inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. 197 of 1950-51.)
SENAGALAGŪḌŪRU, TADPATRI TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in front of the Pārvatī shrine near Śiva temple.
This inscription refers to the rule of Chandiyanna and solar eclipse.

It commences with the praśasti, Sakala-Jagatrayābhivandita, etc. Records the remission,
granted by the chief, of the tax ponna-tere in favour of the mahājanas of Paṇukapaḻu. It is in
characters of about the 9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1952-53.)
SUḶḶA, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone pedestal bearing foot-marks kept near the house of Taṭṭimaniyavaru.

This damaged inscription belongs to the reign of Amōghavarsha. Refers to the foot-marks
(pāduke) and worship of the feet of the guru. Mentions paṭṭagāṟa and the gāvuṇḍa of Uṇkal-30.
It is in characters of about the 9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 290 of 1952-53.)
NĀYAKALLU, KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Four sides of a pillar in front of the Īśvara temple.

This inscription, referring itself to the reign of Akālavarsha Pṛithvivallabha, is dated Śaka
889, Prabhava, solar eclipse. The details correspond to A.D. 967, July 10, Wednesday.

It states that the Vīrabaḷañjas of Naṟavāḍi-500 granted as dēvamāna, specified portions on
every unit of such commodities as areca-nut, turmeric, etc., to god Gavaṟēśvara on the occasion
of a solar eclipse when Nanni-Saḷukki Rāchamalla was ruling. The Baḷañjas are introduced with
a long prasasti.

ra cōḻa pāṁṇḍyaṁ matte maṇḍalika mahā-

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1950-51.)
KYĀSANŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the village.

This fragmentary inscription, referring itself to the reign of Kannara, mentions the authority
of a feudatory of the Chellakēta family over Banavasi and records the death of Chaṭṭayya. This
record is engraved in characters of about the 9-10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1952-53.)
KŌḶIVĀḌ, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragmentary stone bearing sculpture of Jina near the Kalamēśvara temple.

This fragmentary record refers to Khoṭṭigadēva. Seems to commence with the praise of
Jina-śāsana. Mentions the king's subordinate of the Gaṅga family (name lost) bearing the titles
Satyavākya Koṅguṇivarma and Dharmamahārāja. The characters belong to the 10th century A.D.

edition.p: The continuation of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. 54 of 1950-51.)
CHIKKAṆGI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the Śiva temple.

This inscription belonging to the reign of Tailapa is dated Śaka 902, Vikrama, śu. 15,
Sunday, Hastā, Bālavakaraṇa. It records a gift of land by the gāvuṇḍas of the village after washing
the feet of Saṁkayya-Bhaṭṭāra.

l.ka: svadattaṁ parada[tta]ṁ vā yō ·aruni vasundarā ṣa-

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1950-51.)
CHIKKAṆGI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 1) set up near the Śiva temple.

This inscription belonging to the reign of Tailapa is dated Śaka 903, Vishuva, Vaiśākha [śu.]
10, Thursday, Uttarā. The intended date may be A.D. 981, April 16, Saturday, and Uttarā nakshatra
[star].

It states that Chavaṭṭoyya died and his son Chāmuṇḍa set up the memorial stone when
Eṟagāri was holding the office of the Nāḷ-gāmuṇḍa and Eṟechaṭṭa was holding the office of
Urgāmuṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1950-51.)
CHIKKAṆGI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. II) set up near the Śiva temple.

This inscription belonging to the reign of Tailapa is dated Śaka 903, Vishuva, Vaiśākha,
10. Thursday, Uttarā. The date is identical to that of the previous record.

It records the death of gōsāsi Dugaṇa in a cattle-raid, when Eṟegāri was holding the office
of Nāl-gāvuṇḍu and Eṟechaṭṭa was holding the office of Ūr-gāvuṇḍu. Aṇnayya wrote the record.

l.ka: ni • kalla ja • ti • badagoṁdu gavaṁ •

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1950-51.)
HIRĒKAṆGI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone near the dilapidated Nārāyaṇa temple outside the village.

This inscription is dated Śaka year 903, Vishu, Paushya śu. 15, Sunday, Hastā, Vyatīpāta,
Bālavakaraṇa. The given details of date correspond with A.D. 981, December 13. However, other
details do not tally.

This records the death of Chaṭṭayya in an attack by Bīravarma when Kaṇṇapayya was
governing Banavāsi-12000. Eṟagāri was holding the office of Nāḻ-gāvuṇḍu. The mention of “Eṟ-
agāri aynūṟakkam” seems to denote an administrative division of 500 looked after by Nāḷgāvuṇḍa.
Also there is a mention of the administrative division Bāḍa-500 and Piriya-Kaṇnage [modern
Hirekaṇgi] was within this division.

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1950-51.)
KUṆṬAN-HOSAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab from the bed of the tank called Huḍēdahoṇḍa now removed to the village chāvaḍi.

This inscription, referring itself to the reign of Āhavamalla Tailapa, is dated Śaka 906,
Tāraṇa, Paushya śu. 5, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti corresponding to A.D. 984 November 30,
Sunday. The sankranti occurred later.

It records a gift of land presumably for the upkeep of the tank by Eṟagāri, Maṇiyamma
and Pāḍuva Kaliyamma, when Sōbhanarasa was governing the two six-hundred and Banavāsi-12000,
Eṟagāri was holding the office of Nāḷgāmuṇḍu of the Five-hundred villages. Karmmegara Lōkayya
was in charge of Ur-gāmuṇḍu and Pāḍuva Kaliyamma was administering the village.

l.ka: svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasundarā ṣa-
gūravara caṇḍika paṇavaṁ biṭṭa maṅgaḷa || keḷage

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1950-51.)
BAICHAVAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up near the Basavaṇṇa temple.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Tailapadēva is dated in Śaka 902, (wrong
for 907) Pārthiva, Jyēshṭha kṛishṇa 8, Sunday, corresponding to 985 A.D., June 28.

This records the death, in a cattle-raid, of Bīḍi Chaṭṭayya, son of Sivayya who was the
disciple of Āriya Bhaṭāra, when Sōbhanarasa was governor, Eṟegāri was holding the office of
Nāḷ-gāmuṇḍu of the Five-hundred villages, Sōmēśvaradāsi Aniyamma was administering aṇuga-
rājya and Bāradara Ereyamma and Kadugayya were in charge of Ūr-gāmuṇḍu. The memorial
stone was set up by sthānapati Śivarāsi-bhāṭāra. Chāmuṇḍayya composed the record.

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 284 of 1952-53.)
TAḶAṄGERE, KASARAGOD TALUK, KANARA SOUTH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in front of the mosque.

This inscription belongs to the reign of Jayasimha. The details of the date are not given.
It records the gift of a piece of land made by the king to Mōchabbarasī as kanyādāna, i.e. a
gift. States that king Jayasiṁha was born in the lineage of Gautama, Śaradvat, Śantanu, Kṛipa and
Śalya. The land granted is stated to be in the vicinity of Puttūr.

The record belongs to about 10th century A.D. The inscription is published (Ep. Ind. Vol.
XXIX, p. 203).

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1950-51.)
BAICHAVAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the Basavaṇṇa temple.

This inscription, referring itself to the reign of Trailōkyamalladēva, is dated in Śaka 969,
Sarvajit, Bhādrapada Amāvāsyā, Monday, solar eclipse corresponding to A.D. 1047, September 21,
Monday.

It states that the bund of a tank at Baisavaḷḷi which had given way was repaired and an
endowment of wet land made for its upkeep. At that time Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chāvuṇḍarāya was
governing Banavāsi-12000, Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Mayūravarman of the Kadamba family was holding
the fief (manneya sāmya) of Pānuṁgal-500 included in the area and Eṛagāri held the office of Ūr-
gavuṇḍu of Baisavaḷḷi. Chāvuṇḍarāya's younger brother Barmmadēva also made a gift of siddhāya
for the same purpose. Sēnabōva Dāsimayya of Niṭṭasiṁgi wrote the record and Barmōja engraved
it.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1950-51.)
BOMBAY, BOMBAY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Prince of Wales Museum, Slab No. B. B. 309.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Trailōkyamalladēva, is dated in Śaka 976,
Jaya, Vaiśākha Amavāsyā, Monday, solar eclipse correspond to A.D. 1054, May 9, Monday f.d.t. 26.
The eclipse occurred on Tuesday.

It records the construction of shrines of Śāntinātha, Pārśvanātha and Supārśvanātha at
Ponnavāḍa by Chāṅkirāja of the Vāṇasa family an officer of the Chālukya queen Kētaladēvi and
registers grants of lands to these temples by the king at the request of the queen.
Published in Ind-Ant. Vol. XIX, pp. 268 ff and noticed in ASR for 1924-25, p. 115.

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 102 of 1952-53.)
IṬAGI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up inside the Śambhuliṅga temple.

This inscription refers itself to the reign of Trailōkyamalladēva and is dated in Śaka 976,
Jaya, Jyēshṭha, Amāvāsyā, solar eclipse corresponding to 1054 A.D., May 10, Tuesday.

It states that the mahāgrahāra Iṭṭage included in the Kisukāḍu-nāḍu was the native place
of the king's subordinate Śrīpatichamūpa. Describes the Mahājanas of the village and the teacher
Bonteyadēva who belonged to this place and resided at Kanakapura (i.e. Hoṁbaḷ). Registers a
gift of land to the teacher Mallikārjuna, disciple of Bonteyadēva, for god Svayaṁbhu by the
Mahājanas of the place headed by the Ūroḍeya. Also registers gifts to the same god made by
Ūroḍeya Vikkarasa of Kisukāḍu-nāḍu, the residents of Honneganūr and others.

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1951-52.)
HIRĒKERŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. II) set up near Tōṭada Vīraṇṇa temple outside the village.

This inscription, which refers itself to the reign of Trailōkyamalladēva, is dated in Śaka
976, Jaya, Pausha-Amāvāsye, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇasaṁkrānti Vyatīpāta corresponding to A.D. 1054
December 31. However, the week day was Saturday.

It registers a gift of 3 mattal (i.e. mattar) and 13 kammas of guṇiga wet land in the open
space of Hosageṟe and another gift of 400 gadyāṇas for the feeding of the visitors to the village by
Śrīdharakravinta of Chakradhara Bīsavayya of Viśvāmitragōtra, Īśvarayya of Padmanābha-shaḷaṅgi
and Nāraṇa-ghaisāsa, Īśvara-kravinta of Surapati Nāraṇakravinta and Vasudēvayya of Kariya
(black) Sōvaṇṇa-kravinta collectively. These donors who were the sons-in-law (?) of Musukiṭṭa(ṇṇa)
mayya, son Dēvimayya of Āṅgirasagōtra made the above gifts that merit might accrue to their
father-in-law. It appears that the land given as gift was purchased from the Thousand (Mahājanas)
of the Mahāgrāma (village) Piriyakeṟeyūr. These Thousand were conversant with yama, niyama,
svādhyāya, dhyāna etc., which were usual to them.

The record was written by Sōmanātha, son of Gasīyya who was a devotee of all the gods,
brahmins and preceptors. Published in K.I. Vol. IV, No. 25.

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 78 of 1951-52.)
BĀḶAMBĪḌ, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. V) set up near the Vishaharēśvara temple.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Āhavamalladēva is dated Śaka 979 (in
words), Viḷambi, Vaiśākha śu. 3, Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1058, March 29, Sunday, f.d.t. 03.

It describes the Banavāsi country, its illustrious town Mahātaṭāka-grāma and its highly
learned Mahājanas. It also records the gift of the tank area (called kṛishṇa-taṭāka) with the whole
quantity of water in it, after purchase from and with the consent of the Mahājanas for the
exclusive benefit of the deity Aṇḍurēśvara by several persons (whose names are specified). Bhaṭṭa
Vishṇu composed the record. Kāśyapa-Mādhva wrote it handsomely on stone and rūvāri Nāki
engraved it.

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1951-52.)
HIRĒKERŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. I) set up near Tōṭada Viraṇṇa temple outside the village.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Trailōkyamalladēva is dated Śaka 983,
Śārvari, Bhādrapada, Amāvāsya, Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1060 August, 28. But the Cyclic
year Śārvari occurred 982 not 983 as cited in the record.

It records a gift of income from perjuṁka made to certain prominent local residents
(names specified) for the upkeep of the big tank of Piriya-Keṟyūr by Daṇḍanāyaka Guṇḍamayya.
The king's general bears the titles, Narmadā-nady-ubhaya-taṭa-rājahaṁsa (a swan of the two banks
of Narmadā), Māḷava-dhūmakētu (fire to the Māḷavas), Maṇḍavokōṭ-ōllaṁ-ghana (capture of the
Maṇḍava fort), Dhārānagara-kutūhala (a curiosity to the inhabitants of Dhārā) and Mummaṇijaḷadhi-
baḍavānaḷa (submarine fire to the sea of Mummaṇi). Kāśyapa Mādhava wrote the record and
Nāgavarman of Huligeṟe engraved it. It is Published in Karnataka inscriptions Vol. IV, No. 26.

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1952-53.)
BHAṆḌIVĀḌ, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set near the Mallikārjuna temple.

This badly mutilated inscription refers to the rule of Trailōkyamalla. Most of the letters
are effaced; invocatory portion only is extant. It belongs to the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. I) set up near the Grāmadēvata temple.
This damaged inscription refers itself to the reign of Trailōkyamalla.

It states that the king's subordinate Daṇḍanāyaka Mahādēvarasa was governing Banavāsi-12000
and Huligeṟe-300. Contains a description of Māyidēva Daṇḍādhīśa who was a subordinate of the
above. He seems to have belonged to the Sahavāsi tantra of Rāyanārayaṇapura in Kaḷyāna.

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1952-53.)
IṄGALAḶḶI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up at the entrance into the village.

This damaged inscription mentions the king Trailōkyamalla. In characters about 11th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1950-51.)
HIRĒKAṆGI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the debris called Śankarimat6t6i.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Bhuvanaikamalladēva is dated Śaka 993,
Virōdhikṛit, Uttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti, Vyatīpāta, Amāvāse. Monday. These details equate with 1071
A.D. December 25. However, the weekday was Sunday.

The record refers to the construction of the temple of Mūlasthāna-Śaṁkara-Kalidēva by
Chaṭṭikabbe-gāvuṇḍi and registers a gift of land to the same made by her son Chikka-gāvuṇḍa
who was administering the village Piriya-Kaṇṇage, when Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Māvakētarasa was
administering Pānuṁgal-500 and Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Parikantadēva of the Kadamba family was
governing Banavāsi-12000 and Pānuṁgal-500. Bammaya, the sēnabōva of Piriya-Kaṇṇage wrote the
record and Chaṭṭa, son of Mīrijōja engraved it.

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1952-53.)
SAVAṆŪR, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Uḍachavva temple.

This damaged inscription referring itself to the reign of Bhuvanaikamalladēva is dated • •
• • Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrāṇti• It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

It praises the king's subordinates hērisandhivigrahi Daṇḍanāyaka Kēsimayya and Barmadēva.
Mentions the Mahājanas headed by the Ūroḍeya of the agrahāra of Savaṇūru.

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1951-52.)
BĀḶAMBĪḌ, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. II) set up near the Vishaharēśvara temple.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalla, is dated in the Chālukya
Vikrama year 4, Siddhārtha, Pushya, Amāvāsye, Sunday, solar eclipse, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti,
Vyatīpāta corresponding to A.D. 1079, December 26, the weekday was Thursday.

This inscription introduces his Yuvarāja Trailokyamalla. Bīra-Noḷaṁba Jayasiṁhadēva is
described with various epithets such as ruling over Banavase-12000, Sāntaḷige-1000, Puligere-300
and other divisions. Daṇḍanāyaka Baladēvayya is then introduced as holding the perggaḍe-ship.
Ni[di]ya Kētiseṭṭi and Rēvakabbe are stated to have donated with great devotion one guṇiga
mattar land after purchasing it for ten Lokki-gadyāṇa ponnus to god Paṇḍaraṅgēśvara which was
a great magnificient linga [mahādivyaliṅga]. The land was purchased from the Mahājanas of
Piriya-Keṟeyūr represented by Kaṇabba Nāṇayya, Chāvuṇḍayya (son) of Biṭṭayya and Mādhavayya
of Dāsamayya who had assembled on the occasion. The land purchased formerly belonged to
Jannikabbe. Sēnabōva Saṅkimayya wrote the record and [Sātō]ja of Keṟeyūr put in the stone.
Published in K.I. Vol. IV, No. 33.

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1950-51.)
BOMBAY, BOMBAY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Prince of Wales Museum, Slab No. H.B. 8.

This badly damaged inscription probably belonging to the reign period of Vikramāditya VI
is dated Chālukya Vikrama year 3, Siddhārthin, Māgha śu. 3, Sunday, Uttarāyana-saṅkrānti. The
details of date correspond with Sunday, 29th December, 1079 A.D. However, the said saṅkramaṇa
occurred on December 25, Wednesday.

It states that the king's feudatory was Gūvaladēva of the Kadamba family and refers to
the Śaiva teacher Sadyōjāta-Paṇḍita. Composer of the record is Jayadēva. (ASR, p. 99).

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1951-52.)
DOḌVĀḌ, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the market place.

The upper portion of the stone is badly damaged. It dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 4,
Siddhārthi, Kārttika śu 5, Sunday. The details are not regular.
Seems to record a gift by a Mahāsāmanta (details lost).

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1952-53.)
SAVAṆŪR, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying on the tank bund outside the village.

This damaged inscription which refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva is dated
in the Chālukya-Vikrama year 5, Raudri, Chaitra • • • Sunday [Vyati]pāta. The date is irregular.

It registers a gift of land made into the hands of the two hundred Mahājanas of the
agrahāra of Savaṇūr headed by Daṇḍanāyaka Kēsimayya, possibly by the Paṭṭaṇādhipati of
rājadhāni Baṅkāpura, when Mahāpradhāna Hērisandhivigrahi Daṇḍanāyaka (name lost) a faithful
warrior (Pēshaṇa-Hanumān) of Vīra-Noḷaṁba-Pallava Permānaḍi, was governing the agrahāra of
Savaṇūr. Sāntaḷige-Thousand and Nāgarakhaṇḍa-Seventy which comprised a kaṁpaṇa of Banavāsi-
naḍu. Pergaḍe Siṁgimayya wrote the record with chalk on the stone and Rēvavōja incised it.

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 54 of 1952-53.)
KOṆṆŪR, GOKAK TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept in the Jaina temple.

This inscription which refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalla is dated in the
Chālukya-Vikrama year 12, Prabhava, Pausha ba. 14, Vaḍḍavāra, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti corresponding
regularly to A.D. 1087, December 25, Saturday f.d.t. 86.

It records a gift of land, houses, etc., made by Mahāprabhu Nidhiyama-gāvuṇḍa for the
benefit of the Jaina temple constructed by him at Koṁḍanūr. The donor belonged to Balātkāra-
gaṇa of Mūla-saṁgha and the genealogical account of the preceptors is narrated at length. The
Chālukya prince Jayakarṇa and the Raṭṭa chief Sēna are also eulogized. The record is published
in JBBRAS, Vol. X, pp. 287 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1952-53.)
SAVAṆŪR, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab buried near the Nawab's bungalow outside the village.

This fragmentary inscription referring itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalla is dated in
the Chālukya-Vikrama year 12, Prabhava, Jyēshṭha Paurṇima corresponding to A.D. 1087, May 20.

It states that two hundred Mahājanas of the agrahāra of Savaṇūr conferred together and
selected Madhusūdana or Maddiga of the Pārāśara-gōtra and Vājivaṁsa, as the protector of the
frontier town of Savaṇūr.

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1951-52.)
BĀḶAMBĪḌ, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. III) set up near Vishaharēśvara temple.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva is dated in the
Chālukya-Vikrama year 12, Prabhava, Phālguṇa ba. 11, Sunday corresponding to A.D. 1088, February
20, Sunday f.d.t. 14.

It registers a gift of land taken on lease from interest on money deposited with the Thousand
Mahājanas of the mahāgrāma Piriya-Keṟeyūr, who are described with several epithets such as
“Those that had possessed restraint, self-control, self-study, meditation, keeping the mind collected,
silence, principles, etc., who were busy with study, teaching, performing sacrifice and causing to do
so, giving and taking, who were like Brahma in lores like logic, grammar, history, poetry, drama,
critics, whose white fame had reached the ends of all the worlds, who were the royal swan in the
lakes of Nyāya, Vaiśēshika, Lōkāyata, Sāṅkhya, Bauddha and Arhanta (philosophies), who were the
sacred mark on the forehead of the country of Banavasi, an adamantine cage to the resorters, an
elephant to the wicked, givers in abundance, speakers of the same words formally uttered by them,
brave in carrying out their work, destroyers of the root of their enemies, etc., and men endowed
with the virtue of bestowing favour and punishing. These Thousand (Mahājanas) of Piriya-Keṟ
eyūr headed by Sōmayāji, disciple of Vāsudēva, Vāsudēva-Chaṭṭōpādhyāya, Padmanābhayya the
head man (ūroḍeya) of Pervvūr, Kaliya Hattimayya, Hālavuṇḍimayya, Nārayaṇa-Chaṭṭōpādhyāya,
Chēdimayya of Ahitāgni, Dāsayya (son of) Cheṭṭayya Kēśavakravinta of Kōṭiyamma and Sivaṇa-
Kravinta of Gaṅge assembled on the date specified in 11. 20 and 21 and sold to Būtagavuṇḍa son
of Dēvayya one mattar of guṇiga land in Gopavaḷḷi for 8 gadyāṇas. The land was donated by
Būtagāvuṇḍa for food-offering to god Paṇḍaraṅgēśvara.
Sēnabōva Appaṇa wrote the record and Nēmōja engraved it. Pub.: in K.I. Vol. IV, No. 32.

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the compound of the Basavēśvara temple.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva is dated in the
Chālukya-Vikrama year 26, Vishu, Vaiśākha Amāvāsya, Sunday, solar eclipse corresponding to A.D.
1101 April 30, solar eclipse; the weekday was Tuesday.

It records a gift of the village Muriganahaḷḷi to the temple of Brahmēśvara by Daṇḍanāyaka
Gōvindarasa.
Published in Ep. Ind, Vol. V, pp. 214-15.

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the compound of the Basavēśvara temple, below No. 62.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva, is dated in the
Chālukya-Vikrama year 29, Tārana, Bhādrapada śu. 6, Sunday A.D. 1104, August 28, Sunday.
It registers grants made to the Brahmēśvara temple by the inhabitants of Abbalūru.
Published in Ep. Ind, Vol. V, pp. 214-15.

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 288 of 1952-53.)
KOṄGANAPĀḌU KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the diguḍubhāvi.

This inscription is dated Chālukya-Vikrama, Vyaya, Āshāḍha, śu. 12, Thursday, corresponding
to A.D. 1106, June 14, Thursday.

It registers a gift of 2 mattar of land by Mallarasa of Kādūru to Rāmadēva-paṇḍita, son
of Rudrasiri-paṇḍita, for the expenses of worship and offerings to god Bhujaṅgēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1950-51.)
KARGUDARI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing on the tank near the village.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalla is dated in the Chālukya
Vikrama year 33, Sarvadhāri, Herjjuggiya (i.e. Āśvayuja) Puṇṇami, Monday, corresponding to 1108
A.D., September 21.

It states that Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tailapa II of the Kadamba family Banavāse Twelve-
thousand and Hanuṁgal Five-hundred, at his capital of Pārthapura. Tailapa II was governing and
his wife, Bāchaladēvi, made a grant to a temple, which was dedicated jointly to Saṁkhara or Siva,
and Kēsava or Vishnu, and to Bhāskara or the Sun and the inscription opens with an invocation
of the same three gods.
This is published in Indian Antiquary Vol. X, pp. 249 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1950-51.)
BOMBAY, BOMBAY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Prince of Wales Museum Slab No. HB. 7.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva is dated in the
Chālukya-Vikrama year 36, Khara, Vaiśākha Puṇṇami, Monday, Vyatīpāta, lunar eclipse. The
details of date equate with 1111 A.D., April 25.

It records the construction of a temple at Eleyapūruvadavaḷḷi by Māchiyakka, wife of
Mahāsāmantādhipati Daṇḍanāyaka Muṭṭigalla-Chaṭṭōpādhyāya. States that the village was being
governed by Malayamatīdēvi who is described as the piriyarasi of the king. Reviewed in ASR
for 1936-37, p. 99.

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1950-51.)
RĀMATĪRTHA, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing near the Rāmēśvara temple.

This damaged inscription which refers to the Tribhuvanamalladēva is ruling from Jayantipura
is dated the Vikrama year 3[6], Nandana, Pushya śu 1, Monday solar eclipse, Uttarāyana-Saṁkranti.
The date is irregular.

Registers gifts of land to the god Rāmēsvara for daily worship etc., made over to the
hands of the teacher Rāmēśvara of Rāmatīrtha by certain gāvuṇḍas (names specified) and the
Seventy of the village.

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 74 of 1951-52.)
BĀḶAMBĪḌ, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. I) set up near Vishaharēśvara temple.

This fragmentary inscription is dated in Chālukya-Vikrama year 42, Viḷaṁbi, Śrāvana ba.
2, Sunday corresponding to A.D. 1118, August 4, Sunday f.d.t 32.
Mentions the Mahāgrāma Piriya-Keṟeyūru.

l.ka: ccāḷukya vikramakālada 42 neya viḷaṁbisaṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa ba 2 ·ādityavāra

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1952-53.)
KOṆṆŪR, GOKAK TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept in the Jaina temple.

This damaged inscription referring itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva is dated
in the Chālukya-Vikrama year 46, Plava, Pushya śu. 13. These details correspond to A.D. 1121,
December 23, Friday.

It registers a gift made to the sage Vāsupūjya for the benefit of the Jaina temple
constructed by Jayakarṇa the beloved son of Vikramāchāri. The record is published in JBBRAS,
Vol. X, pp. 287 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. 240 of 1950-51.)
PARLA, KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two broken stones near the cattle pound.

This inscription which refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva, ruling from the
capital Kalyāṇa, is dated the Chālukya-Vikrama year 49, Krōdhi, Pushya ba [2], corresponding
probably to A.D. 1125, December, 28 Monday.
It refers to Ghaṭeyaṅkakāra-Chōḷa-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. 79 of 1951-52.)
HIRĒKERŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. I) set up on the tank-bund.

This inscription refers to Chālukya Vikrama year [50], Viśvāvasu, Mārgaśira-māsa, śu. 9,
Wednesday, corresponding to A.D. 1125, November 5, Wednesday.

It records the death of Sātiga son of Bhojanganāraṇayya of Piriyakeṟeyūru in a cattle raid.
The deceased hero is euologised as biṭṭērukāraragaṇḍa, maledumachcharisuvaragaṇḍa Paranāriputra
etc. This memorial was carved by Mākōja.

harivalli sattu śva¨rggastanāda matta sātiga biruda biṭṭēṟukāṟada-

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. 79 of 1950-51.)
MŪḌŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up near the Barmadēva temple.

This inscription is dated in the Chālukya-Vikrama kāla 50, Parābhava, Nija-Chaitra śu 1,
Sunday. The date is irregular.

It records the death of Gommiseṭṭi of Mūḍevūr in a fight when a Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
(name not specified) was ruling over Banavāsi-12000.

l.ka: ya parābhavasaṁvatsa[ra] nija caitra suddha 1 ·ādivāradaṁdu mū-
sattanu maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(A.R. No. 239 of 1950-51.)
PARLA, KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone near the Īśvara temple.

This damaged inscription referring itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladeva is dated in
the cyclic year [Vi]kāri, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. The date is not verifiable.
It seems to record a donation made by Ballaya Chōḷa-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1952-53.)
HŪVINA-HAḌAGALLI, HADAGALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of a stone near a tank, now kept at the Inspection Bungalow.

This fragmentary record contains only portions of the king's praśasti and parts of the
culogy of a Mahāsāmanta. The characters belong to about 11th-12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1951-52.)
DOḌVĀḌ, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in Yamōji's lane.

This damaged and worn out inscription mentions only a few titles of the Chālukya
praśasti. This is in characters of about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 44 of 1951-52.)
HOMBAḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone kept in the Saṅkaraliṅga temple.

This badly damaged and worn out record refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalla.
Seems to record a gift of land.

l.ka: svasti samastabhuvanāśraya śrīpṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēśvara paramabha-

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1951-52.)
HOMBAḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Door-jambs of the Hanumān temple near the village.

This fragmentary and worn out inscription is not clear. It belongs to 12th century A.D.
Mentions the [Hundred] and Twenty [mahājanas], Avadhūtadēva, Kriyāśakti and Vāmadēva-paṇḍita.

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 80 of 1950-51.)
MŪḌŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone lying near the Barmadēva temple Stone No. 5.

This worn out inscription in the reign of Tribhuvanamalla contains a eulogy of
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Singarasa and refers to the rule of Mahāmaṇḍalēsvara (name not specified) of
the Kadamba family over Banavāsi-12000. Mentions Kammāra Chāvuṇḍa. The record was written
by Bālachandra-paṇḍita.

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1951-52.)
BAILAHOṄGAL, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the backyard of Gadigeyya Ēṇigimaṭha.

This worn out inscription refers to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva and the date is lost,
except Thursday.

It refers to the feudatory chief Mahāsāmanta Aṅka of the Raṭṭa family, Śāntiyakka and
the Kūṁḍi province. Describes the Jaina teacher Jinadēva-sūri of the Yāpanīya-saṁgha, Maiḷāpa-
anvaya and Kāraya-gaṇa, to which Muḷḷa-bhaṭṭāraka and others belonged. Seems to record a gift
of land to a Jaina temple.

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(A.R. No. 103 of 1952-53.)
RŌN, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragmentary stone lying near the Kalamēśvara temple.

This undated fragmentary inscription refers to the reign of Tribhuvanamalla ruling from
Kalyana. Only the praśasti of the king is preserved. On palaeographic grounds the record would
appear to be of the time of Vikramāditya VI.

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1950-51.)
RĀMATĪRTHA, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing near the Rāmasvāmi temple.

This damaged inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya
VI). The date is insufficient for verification. Refers to the authority of the Kadamba feudatory
Sāntayadēva over Banavāsi-12000 and Pānuṁgal-500. Mentions his queen (name lost).

l.ka: ṁbhāya saṁbhavē || svasti samastabhuvanāśrayaṁ śrī prithuvīvallabha mahā-

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1951-52.)
KALGHAṬGI, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone lying by the side of the tank called Rustum Sāb's tank near the village.

This fragmentary inscription refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva. It states
that Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gūvaladēva was governing Uṇukal-Thirty and Sarbbe-Thirty. Mentions his
subordinate Chaṭṭayadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1951-52.)
HIRĒKERŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing near the Girls School.

This badly damaged inscription referring itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalla is dated
in Chālukya-Vikrama year (lost), Chaitra, śu. 5, Thursday.

It describes the learned Mahājanas of the village. Seems to record a gift of land by
several individuals.

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 78 of 1950-51.)
MŪḌŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Rāmēśvara temple.

This inscription which refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva ruling from the
capital Jayantipura is dated Bhūlōka-varsha 12, Piṁgaḷa, Phālguna ba. 10, Thursday corresponding
to A.D. 1138, February 10.

It records a gift of land for the offerings etc., to the god Mallikārjuna by Sōvagāvuṇḍa
of Mūḍeyūr, situated in Eḍevoḷal-70, when Vira-Kadamba Mallikārjuna, son of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Tailaha of the Kaḍaṁba family was governing Banvāse-12000 and Panuṁgal-500. The gift was
entrusted to Boppejiya. Sēnabōva Paṇḍita who handed over a sthāna to his son (i.e., disciple)
Mallikārjunajīya. Sēnabova Sarvadēvaṇṇa wrote the record and Māḷeya engraved it.
inscription.h4: On the top of the figures.

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 87 of 1950-51.)
SHIRGŌḌ, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone near the Īśvara temple.

This inscription which refers itself to the reign of Pratāpachakravarti Jagadēkamalla is
dated in the 6th year of his reign, Rudhirōdgāri, Kārtika ba 5, Monday. The details agree for
Dundubhi in 1142 A.D., November 9.

It records the death of a person (name lost) who was the son of Masaṇeya and Māḷauve
and also the son-in-law of Lōkagāvuṇḍa of Sirigōḍu in a fight.

l.ka: ¨rṣada 6 neya rudhirōdgārisaṁvatsarada kā¨rtti[ka] bahuḷa paṁcami sōmavāradaṁdu

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. 86 of 1950-51.)
SHIRGŌḌ, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone near the Īśvara temple outside the village.

This inscription, which refers to the reign of pratāpachakravarti Jagadēkamalla, is dated in
the 5th year of his reign, Dundubhi, Vaisākha Sunday. The details are very irregular. It records
the death of Siṁgōja, son of kammāra Kētōja, in a raid on the village.

l.ka: • • ṭṭida magaṁ siṁgōja nūruvesakke pōgi ma-

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1950-51.)
KARGUDARI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab standing S. No. 80.

The record refers to the regnal year of pratāpachakravarti Jagadēkamalladēva and is dated
Prabhava, Pushya, śu. 1, Monday, Dakshiṇāyana (mistake for Uttarāyana saṁkramana). These details
correspond with Thursday, December 25, 1147 A.D.

It records a gift of land for offerings etc., in the temple of Vijaya-Pārśva-Jinēṁdra
constructed at Kaṟeguduṛe by Mahāvaḍḍabhyavahāri Kalliseṭṭi, when Mahāprachaṇḍa-daṇḍanāyaka
Sōvarasa was administering Hānuṁgal-500 and Maṇḍalēśa Taila of the Kadamba family was ruling
over a part of the territory. The gift was entrusted to the teacher Nāgachandra-bhaṭṭāraka, pupil
of Hariṇandi, [himself] a disciple of Vāsupūjya. These teachers were descendants of Pūjyapāda and
belonged to the Sūrastha gaṇa and Chitrakūṭa-anvaya.

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1950-51.)
KUṆTAN-HOSAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept near the Basavaṇṇa temple.
This mutilated inscription refers to 10th regnal year of Jagadēkamalla, Prabhava.

It registers a gift of land by Nāgiseṭṭi and others to a Jaina deity. Mentions Āchaladēvi
and the Kadamba chief Taila-maṇḍalēsa.

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1950-51.)
SHIRGŌḌ, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone near a well called Kālbhāvi.

This inscription which refers itself to the reign of pratāpachakravarti Jagadēkamalla is
dated in 12th year, Prabhava. Bhādrapada ba 10, Monday. The details are irregular.
It records the death of Kīrtiyamma-seṭṭi.

l.ka: kī¨rttiyammaseṭṭi taṁna magaḷu kaggāvevuṁ tānuṁ baḍiya hiḍi-

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 236 of 1950-51.)
LADDAGIRI, KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone near the ruins of the Rāmappa temple.

This damaged inscription refers itself to the reign of Jagadēkamalla. It seems to register
a gift made by Suṅkaverggaḍes Ajja • • • • • karasarāja and Dēvaparāja to god Rāmēsvara.
In characters of 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 96 of 1951-52.)
RAṬṬIHAḶḶI, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing near the Kadaṁbēśvara temple.

This long record in beautiful Kannaḍa prose and verse is unfortunately damaged. The
inscription commences with the invocatory verses in praise of lord Śiva and gives a genealogical
account of the Chālukyas down to pratāpachakravarti Jagadēkamalla II, the ruling king. He
entrusted the administration of the Banavāsi country to his officer, manneveggaḍe, haḍapavaḷa
Bammideva-daṇḍādhīśa who, however, transferred it to his younger brother Mādhava-chamūnātha.

After eulogising Raṭṭahaḷḷi, the chief town of Nūṟuṁbāḍa, a division of one Hundred,
which was a kaṁpaṇa of Banavāsināḍu, the record gives a genealogical account of the chiefs of
this division who belonged to Kadamba family.

The record then refers to the minister of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kētarasa, viz. Sōmanātha or
Sōvaṇṇa. He was the son of Bhāskarārya and Māḷikavve of Atri-gōtra and had two elder brothers
Rāyarasa, and Malla. Malla held the office of sandhivigrahi while Sōvaṇa was the mantri.

The continuation of the record from here is lost since the engraved slab is broken and
lost.

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1950-51.)
GŌLIHAḶḶI, KHANAPUR TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near Siddhaliṅga temple.

This damaged record refers to the reign of Bhulōkamalla Sōmēśvara IV and states that
Permāḍidēva, the Kadaṁba chief of Goa and his mother Maiḷaladēvī were ruling from their
capital at Gōve. The record is dated the Kaliyuga year 4283, Manmatha, Phālguṇa śu 13, Thursday.
The details of date are very irregular. The given tithi in the Kaliyuga year 4283, corresponded
to 1183 A.D., February 7, Monday, when the cyclic year was Śubhakṛit. If we consider the cyclic
year, the details would correspond to 1176 A.D., February 24, Tuesday. It is difficult to identify
Bhulōkamalla of this record.

The earlier portion of the record which gives a genealogical account of the Kadaṁbas
of Goa refers also to Vijayāditya, the younger brother of Permāḍi, as ruling along with his
brother. It further discloses the fact that Maiḷalamahādēvī was the daughter of Malayamatīdēvī
and Vikramāditya VI.

It registers gifts of land, house-site and oilmill by Malliseṭṭi, Dēviseṭṭi and Madhuraseṭṭi
sons of Gangiseṭṭi to the basadi got built by the latter at Kiru Saṁpagāḍi. It also registers gifts of
income from taxes etc. by the Five hundred. These gifts were made over to Vāsūpūjya-bhaṭṭāraka,
of Mulasaṁgha and Baḷagāragaṇa, disciple of Nēmichandra-bhaṭṭāraka whose lineage is described.
The gifts were made on the occasion of a lunar eclipse. The record refers to a mahāpradhāna,
haḍapavaḷa etc., of the Kadaṁba chiefs. But his name is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1952-53.)
GOKAK FALLS, GOKAK TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This worn out inscription is dated in Śaka 1093, Vikṛiti, Māgha Paurṇima. Further details
of the date are lost. It records a gift for the repairs etc., of the temple, when Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Kattamadēva of the Raṭṭa family was governing Kūṁḍi Three Thousand. Seems to praise the
exploits of the reigning king who is said to have vanquished the Chōḷa and Pāṇḍya kings and
others. Details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1952-53.)
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This damaged inscription refers itself to the reign of Kalachurya-chakravarti Bhujabalamalla.
The Śaka year is lost. The extant details are Paushya śu. 11, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa saṅkramaṇa.

It records the grant of the village Koṁḍanūr situated in the Kuṁdurge-kaṁpaṇa of Kūṁḍi
Three Thousand for offerings etc., to god Taṭēśvara of Mahātīrtha by the king's officer Pergaḍe
Dhannugidēva in conjunction with the Karṇas, with the consent of the king. Amṛitarāsi was the
recipient of the gift.

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1952-53.)
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This badly damaged record refers to Tribhuvanamalla Sōvidēva. It is dated in Śaka 1093,
Vi[kṛiti], Kārttika śu. 5, Thursday, corresponding regularly to A.D. 1170, October 15, f.d.t. 19. The
Śaka year was current. It records a gift for the offerings etc., of the god Taṭēśvara by the king's
officer Sōvaṇa and Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kattamadēva who went to the place in person.

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1951-52.)
RAṬṬIHAḶḶI, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. I) kept in Madhy-maṇḍapa of the Kadaṁbēśvara temple.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Rāyamurāri Sōvidēva is dated in Śaka 1097,
Jaya, Jyēshṭha Amāvāsya, solar eclipse, Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1174, June 2, the tithi ended
at • 33 the previous day when there was a solar eclipse.

The record registers a gift of gold, a row of houses, oil mill etc., made into the hands of
the divine Kalyāṇaśakti for the offerings to the god and repairs to the temple of Kaḍaṁbēśvara by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Pāṇḍyadēva and his subordinate Chauṇḍarasa. Describes the Banavāsi country,
its kaṁpaṇa Nūṟuṁbāḍa with the chief town Raṭṭahaḷḷi and the genealogy of the chiefs ruling
over the latter to whose family Pāṇḍyadēva belonged.

The epigraph eulogies Raṭṭapaḷḷi and its chiefs. The name of the author of this record is
given in the last line, but it is unfortunately damaged and lost. The extant portion refers to • • •
dēvarasa. The name of the engraver is also lost.

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. II) set up in the Basavēśvara temple.

This damaged inscription refers to reign of Sōmēśvara and the extant details of date are
Chaitra, Sunday. Seems to record death of Bīra-(gāmuṇḍa) of Abbalūr in a fight.

l.ka: ḷiya bāja ba • pu • • ṟūṟara kuḍuna • • • • • • • • • • tra • • •

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 80 of 1951-52.)
HIRĒKERŪR, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. II) set up on the tank-bund.

This badly damaged inscription belongs to the 7th regnal year of Siṁghanadēva and the
cyclic year Dhātu. Seems to record the death of a person probably in a fight, the clue for which
can be obtained in the two wors (line 12)-“Jitēna labhyate” of a verse which intends to encourage
soldiers to fight.
Rest of the information is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. V) set up in the Basavēśvara temple.

This badly damaged inscription is dated in the 1[1]th year Pramādi (Irregular). The month
and the date are lost.
It records the death of a warrior in a fight; details are not clear; mentions Abbalūr.

l.ka: • • ḍeya • • vāyaṁ • • • du vūlida ·āga • dama • ya ·ārike • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. III) set up in the Basavēśvara temple.

This inscription is dated on the full moon day, coupled with Monday of the Pramāthi,
Āshāḍha Paurṇimā, Monday, one of the years of Singhaṇa. The date is irregular. The given tithi
ended, not on a Monday, but on Saturday, 29th June, A.D. 1219.

It records the death of two warriors Mācha and Gōma in a cattle-raid at Abbalūru, led
by Īśvaradēva of Beḷagavatti when the feudatory Siṅghaṇadēva was governing Banavāsi-12000.
Published in Ep. Ind, Vol. V, pp. 261 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Māsti stone (No. 2) set up in the Basavēśvara temple.

This damaged inscription is dated in the 26th year of the reign of Siṁgaṇadēva, Jaya
(details lost). It records the death probably of a lady (details lost).

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. 241 of 1950-51.)
PARLA, KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in the field of Kuruba Rāmanna on the border of the village.

This inscription belonging to the reign of Siṁhaṇadēva, is dated in the cyclic year Vyaya,
Śravana śu. 5, Thursday corresponding to A.D. 1226, July 30, Thursday f.d.t. 13.
It registers a gift in the regime of Mahāpradhāna Sāraṁga-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1952-53.)
AMBE JŌGĀI, BIG DISTRICT, HYDERABAD.
inscription.p: Near Mominabad, Big District.

Records a gift of a village in Āṁradēsa for the benefit of god Sakalēśvara by the king's
general Khōlēśvara of the Maudgala-gōtra. Published in the sources of the Medieval History of
the Dekkan (Marāthi) Vol. I, pp. 55 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1951-52.)
BĀḶAMBĪḌ, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. IV) set up in the Vishaharēśvara temple.

This damaged inscription referring to Siṁgaṇadeva is dated Śaka year 1150, Sarvadhāri,
Chaitra, su. [15], Monday corresponding to A.D. 1228, March 20, Monday.

It praises the deity called Paṁḍaragenātha or Paṁḍaraṁga. Describes the king's sarvādhikāri
Āriya Malliseṭṭi, his son whose name is lost, Baṁdaṇikeya Bammidēva and the Thousand
Mahājanas of Piriya-Keṟavur. Seems to record a gift (details lost) made in favour of the deity
by Daṇṇāyaka Bāchaya into the hands of the divine Brahmarāsi.

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Tōṭadappana maṭha.

This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 1157, Manmatha, Śrāvaṇa ba. Amāvāsya, solar
eclipse. The details correspond to A.D. 1235, August 15, Wednesday.

Records a gift of land to the Gaṅgādhara-bhaṭṭa, Harihara-bhaṭṭa, Nākaṇa-bhaṭṭa, Kalli-
bhaṭṭa, Yisvara-bhaṭṭa, Maneyadhara-bhaṭṭa, Kēsava-bhaṭṭa, Aditya-bhaṭṭa, Pavadēva-bhaṭṭa, Bhāskara-
bhaṭṭa, Māyidēva-bhaṭṭa, Vāmana-bhaṭṭa Vāmana-paṇḍita, Mādhava-bhaṭṭa, Rāya-bhaṭṭa, Bhaṭṭōpādhya,
Purushōtama-bhaṭṭa, Māyidēva-bhaṭṭa for the prosperity of the king's rule and their own family by
Ponna-Bommiseṭṭi and Chauḍiseṭṭi, when Mahāsāmantādhipati Mahāpradhāna Mahāmātya Malliseṭṭi
was governing Beḷovla Three Hundred, Puligere Three Hundred and Banavāsi Twelve Thousand.
The gift was entrusted to the Mahājanas of Bērpaṭṭi, whose virtues are described.

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1951-52.)
RAṬṬIHAḶḶI, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the Mukhamaṇḍapa of the Kadaṁbēśvara temple lower portion.

This epigraph doesnot refer to any ruling king. It is dated in the cyclic year Hēmaḷambi,
Phālguna Amāvāsya, solar eclipse, Sunday. The details of the date given are irregular. In the cyclic
year corresponding to 1237-38 A.D. a solar eclipse occurred only in the month of Mārgaśira, on
1237 A.D. December 19, Saturday; Phālguṇa Amāvāsya occurred on Wednesday, 17th March, 1238
A.D.

The record registers a gift of lands, and house to God Mallināthadēva by mahāpradhāna
Karaṇada Kallarasa when Mahādēvadaṇnāyaka was administering Banavāsi-12000. It further records
gifts of tolls on merchandise by the Baṇanjigas, Nakharas, Mummuridaṇḍas and the Thousand
parivāra including the Sandhivigrahi. The grants were made over to Mūṟujāvidēva.

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1951-52.)
RAṬṬIHAḶḶI, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the Mukhamaṇḍapa of the Kadaṁbēśvara temple.

This inscription referring to Siṁghaṇadēva is dated Śaka 1161, Viḷaṁbi, Āshāḍha śu. 15,
[lunar] eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1238, June 28.

It registers the gift of the toll income on eleven bullocks to god Kaḍambēśvara for
burning a perpetual lamp, by Gubbiya Kalleya (Karaṇada Kalla), the officer in charge of taxes.
He was the son of Chāvaṇa and Māḷave, and a minister of Honnabamma. The gift was made
over to Mūṟujāvidēva. It also registers a gift of land to the god by Bārika Bīreya.

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1951-52.)
RAṬṬIHAḶḶI, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. II) kept Madhyamaṇḍapa of the Kadaṁbēśvara temple.

This inscription, referring itself to the reign of Siṁghaṇadēva, is dated Śaka 1160, Viḷaṁbi,
Mārgaśira Amāvāsyā, Monday, solar eclipse corresponding to A.D. 1238, December 8. The weekday,
however, was Wednesday.

This inscription refers to the rule of Siṅghaṇa II, whose grandfather and father Bhillama II
and Jaitugi I respectively, are also eulogized. Siṅghaṇa's subordinate was Ārya Malla or Malliseṭṭi
who at his masters instance, proceeded against Nārasiṁha II and seized from him fourteen
elephants. This Narasiṁha is identical with the Hoysaḷa king of that time. The record gives the
genealogy of this general who was the son of Bomma. Malla's sons were Rēva, Honnabomma,
Chāvuṇḍa and Achala. The epigraph then praises the achievements of Honnabomma who had his
capital of Muḷugunda. His son was Gupta Malla.

The inscription refers to Nūṟumbāḍa in Banavāsināḍu with Raṭṭapaḷḷi as its capital, which
was being governed by the chiefs of the Kaḍamba family whose genealogy is also given. Kētarasa
III of this family had two sons whose names are revealed in this record as Garuḍapāṇḍya and
Vīrapāṇḍya.

It also contains a genealogical account of the preceptors of the Kāḷamūkha sect, Parvatāvaḷi
and Kiṭṭagāveanvaya. The record states that Honnabommiseṭṭi had the deities Mallēśvara and
Rājēśvara installed within the temple of Kaḍaṁbēśvara and registers a gift of the village Kaḍavūr,
other lands etc. The gift was made over to rājaguru Mūṟujāvidēva disciple of Lakūḷiśa.

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. 42 of 1951-52.)
HURḶIKUPPI, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up near the Īśvara temple.

This badly damaged inscription is dated in the cyclic year Hēmaḷambi in the reign of
[Siṁ] gaṇadēva and Thursday. Other details are lost.
It seems to record the death of • • • chūriya Navileya-nāyaka in a fight.

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1950-51.)
ARḶEŚVAR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the Hanumān temple.

This damaged inscription is dated in the 15th year of the reign of [Kanna]radēva, Raudri,
Bhādrapada ba. 14, Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1260, September 5, Sunday f.d.t. 08.
It records the death of a person in a fight. Mentions Yaḷeyasirivura.

l.ka: da ba 14 ·ā • yaḷeya sirivūra • maragā[vuṁḍa]nu naṭṭa

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1951-52.)
HIRĒKERŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. III) set up on the tank-bund.

This damaged and worn out inscription belongs to Rāmachandrarāya's regnal year 15,
Chitrabhānu, Bhādrapada, • • • Sōmavāra. The date will correspond to some Monday between 6th
August and 3rd September in A.D. 1282. In the absence of the tithi it is difficult to be precise on
this point.

It refers to a battle fought between Nāḷprabhu • • • dēvarasa, the manneya of Bandaḷike
and • • (name lost) of Tarakū• It is stated that a person (name lost) ascended heaven during the
fight.

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 74 of 1950-51.)
MALLIGGĀR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Māsti stone set up in the Siddhēśvara temple.

This damaged inscription, referring itself to the reign of Rāmachandra's regnal year 18, is
dated in Sarvadhāri, Paushya su. 5, Wednesday corresponding to 1288 A.D. December 29.

This record mentions god Svayaṁbhū Tailēśvara of Hānuṁgal and seems to record the
death of a person in a fight.

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1951-52.)
RAṬṬIHAḶḶI, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone built into the wall of the Kadambēśvara temple near the river.

This badly damaged and worn out record belonging to the reign of Rāmachandra refers
to the death of a hero. It mentions Malayanāyaka; refers to an expedition led by maṇḍalēśvara
Ācharasapaṇḍita, the general, described as the maneya pradhāna of the king Rāmachandradēva. He
appears to belong to the Sāḷuva family. All other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 79 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the Hanumān temple.

This damaged inscription is dated in the 8th regnal year of Vīra-Ballāḷadēva, [Sārvari]
Śobhakrit, Pushya paurṇimā, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa. The date is irregular.

It records the gift of land and house-sites to Vāmadēva-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya, Kēsavaghaisāsaru,
Maṅgaḷamaḷḷi-bhaṭṭaru, Aṇṇegēṟeya Māchi-bhaṭṭaru, Chakrapāṇi-bhaṭṭōpādhyāyaru, Sōmēśvara-
bhaṭṭa, Nāgadēva-bhaṭṭaru, Mallidēva-bhaṭṭaru, Channakēśava-bhaṭṭaru, Nārasiṁha-bhaṭṭaru, Kāḷidāsa-
ghaisāsaru etc by Sōvaṇṇa Vāmarasa, Mahāprabhu Chandarasa and samastraprajegalu. Madhusū
dana or Madhuva-chamūpa is praised.

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1952-53.)
KURUVATTI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the capital of a pillar to the right in the Mallikārjuna temple.

This inscription is dated in the 15th year of the reign of Vīraballāḷa, Raktākshi, Vaiśākha
Puṇṇame, Monday, solar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1204, April 15, Thursday (not Monday).

It registers a gift of 2 guṇigana-mattar of land to god Āhumallēśvara by the Mahājanas
headed by the Ūroḍeyas, etc of the village Kureti. The gift was entrusted into the hands of
rājaguru Lōkābharaṇadēva.

l.ka: svasti hoysaṇa vīraballāḷa • • va¨rṣada 15 neya raktaṁ-
ḍagaṇa tuṁgake paḍuva hola sasadēvara • yiṁ teṁka śrīmadrājaguru lōkā-
bharaṇadēvara kālaṁka¨rcci dhārāpū¨rvvakaṁ māḍikoṭṭa gadde guṇigana mattaru 2

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Hirēmaṭha.

The damaged inscription is dated in Śaka 1130, Vibhava, Māgha, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The
date is irregular [A.D. 1209.]

It registers a grant of land and a house-site by one Daṇḍanāyaka (name not clear). It
registers a gift of 4 mattars of land to god Kēśava and also gift of a house. Describes the learned
persons.

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Chāvaḍi.
Palaeographically the record may be assigned to the 12th century A.D.

It stops abruptly after stating that the king's subordinate Mahāpradhāna Sēnādhipati
Bāhattaraniyōgādhipati Kumāra-Paṇḍitayya was administering Veḷuvala Three Hundred.

l.ka: gaṁḍa gaṁḍabhēruṁḍa kadanapracaṁḍa sanivārasiddhi giridu¨rggamalla ca-

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. IV) set up in the Basavēśvara temple.

This damaged inscription referring itself to the reign of Harihara I is dated Śaka [1271]
Sarvadhāri, Phālguna śu. 1, Thursday. (A.D. 1349, February 19).

It records the death of a warrior in a fight (detail not clear) when the king's officer
Samudra-saḷuva Vīra-Bāchappadēva was administering the tract.

l.ka: phālguna śu 1 gu śrī vīraharihararāyanu pruthvīrājyaṁ geyu-

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1950-51.)
SHIRGŌḌ, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone with sculpture called Komara Rāma.

This inscription dated Śaka year 1352, Sādhārana, Kā[rtika] • • • • • Wednesday, records the
death of Jambe-Nāyaka, son of Harige-Nāyaka, in a fight. The hero's two wives Kāmeva-Nāyakiti
and Lakava-Nāyakiti also died after him.

l.ka: ga vādalli • • hārigenāykara maga jeṁbenāyka sattu suralōkaprāptanāda [maṁ]-

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. 96 of 1952-53.)
SHIRAGUPPI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Wooden beam in the Huḍēda Hanumappa temple.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Kṛishṇarāya is dated in the cyclic year
Bahudhānya, Phālguṇa śu. 10 corresponding to A.D. 1519, February 9, Wednesday.

It states that the temple was built for the merit of Tirumalappa, an officer of Bāgila
Timmappa-nāyaka, by his son-in-law Ayirāpiḷa. In characters of about 16th century A.D.

l.ka:
bahudānya saṁvatsarada pālguṇa śuddha 10 ralū śrī kṛṣṇarāyara kāladalū
bāgila tiṁmappanāyakara-

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1951-52.)
RAṬṬIHAḶḶI, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. I) lying outside the Kadaṁbēśvara temple.

This damaged inscription is dated Śaka 1446, Plavaṁga, Vaiśākha. Other details of the date
are not given. It seems to refer to a gift to an individual of Yaju-śākha.

l.ka: hasya dauṣṭrādaṁḍassa pātu vaḥ hēmādri kalaśā yatra dhā trīchatraśriyaṁ

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. 48 of 1950-51.)
BAICHAVAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in front of the Hanuman temple.

This damaged inscription referring itself to the reign of Vīra-Achyutarāya-Mahārāya is
dated Śaka 1457, Manmatha, Āshāḍha ba. 10 corresponding to A.D. 1535, June 25, Friday.

It seems to record a gift of the village [Bai]chavaḷi to the local deity for the merit of
the king and his parents by his subordinate Tirupatyaya who was stationed at Baṁkāpura.

l.ka: naśakavaruṣa 1457 neya manmathasaṁvatsarada ·āśāḍa ba 10 lū śrīmanmahārājā-

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 48 of 1951-52.)
HOMBAḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the barber's lane.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Sadāśivarāya is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṁga,
Āshāḍha śu. 11, Wednesday. But for the week day the details correspond to A.D. 1547, June 28.
The weekday, however, was Tuesday.

It records the remission of tax on barbers by the king in the Toṟugali-sīme at the instance
of Mallōja and Chaṁdōja.

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1951-52.)
RAṬṬIHAḶḶI, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. II) lying in the Kadaṁbēśvara temple.

This inscription is badly damaged and worn out. It is dated Śaka 147[1], other details being
lost. The date cannot be verified. Seems to record a gift (details lost).

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. 87 of 1951-52.)
HIRĒKERŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the tank close to the village.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Vīrapratāpa Sadāśivarāya is dated Śaka
1472, Sādhāraṇa, Jyēshṭha ba. 5, which may probably be corresponding to Friday, May 23, A.D.
1550. The cyclic year on this day was Piṅgaḷa and not Sādhāraṇa as mentioned in the record.

It records a gift of taxes such as siddhāya and tax on houses, gifts, marriages, on
Mahānavami (festival) to barbers Timmōja, Koṁḍōja and Bhadrōja. This sort of privilege was given
to the barbers in the whole kingdom of Sadāśivarāya. The gift was recognized as sarvamānya in
the locality (sīme) of Koḍa.

l.ka: ṁ saṁbhavē svasti jayābhyudayaśālivāhana śakavaruśa 1472 neya sādhāraṇa saṁ-
ṁ vatsarada jēṣṭa ba 5 lū śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara kaṭhāri-

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(A.R. No. 91 of 1951-52.)
RAṬṬIHAḶḶI, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. III) lying outside the Kadaṁbēśvara temple.

This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 146 • the other details being lost• The date cannot
be verified. Mentions Gōve, Banavāse and Raṭṭehaḷḷi and Kaṭṭehaḷḷi-sīme.

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1951-52.)
NĒSARGI, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built as support in the temple called Jōḍi-guḍi outside the village.

This inscription, referring itself to the reign of Kārtavīrya IV, is dated in Śaka 1141,
Bahudhānya, Māgha Śu. 7, Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, Vyatīpāta corresponding to A.D. 1219,
January 24, Thursday. It records gifts to the temples of Habbēśvara, Māṇikēśvara and Siddhēśvara,
erected by Bācheya-nāyaka and his wife Māyidēvi at Nēsaṟige. The record is published in
JBBRAS, Vol. X, p. 240 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. 44 of 1950-51.)
ARḶĒŚVARA, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Front side of the makaratōraṇa of the Kadaṁbēśvara temple.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Śāntivarmadēva is dated in the Chālukya-
Vikrama kāla 13, Vibhava, Māgha Śu. 10, Monday. The date corresponds to A.D. 1089, January 24,
Wednesday.

It records that Rāmasiṅgi-paṇḍita, who was administering the village Aḷayasirivūr situated in
Eḍevoḷal-70, got installed the god Kadambēśvara. Banavāsi-12000 and Pānuṁgal-500 are mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. 45 of 1950-51.)
inscription.p: Same pillar.

This inscription referring itself to the reign of Tailapadēva (son of Śāntivarma) is dated
in the Chālukya-Vikrama kāla 52, Plavaṁga, Vaiśakha śu. 10, Thursday. The details of date equate
with A.D. 1127, April 23, Saturday.

It records a gift of land for offerings and repairs by Tribhuvana-paṇḍita, son (i.e. disciple)
of Rāmasiṅgi-paṇḍita who extended the Kaḍambēśvara temple making it five shrined and adding
the maṇḍapa. The chief was governing Banavāsi-12000 and Hānuṁgal-500.

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1950-51.)
ARḶĒŚVAR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar on the proper left side of the Kadambēśvara temple.

This inscription is dated in the 60th year of the reign of Vīra-Mallikārjunadēva, Rākshasa,
Jyēshṭha śu. 15, Monday. The date is irregular.

It records a gift of land to god Kaḍaṁbēśvara at Aḷayasiriyūr situated in Eḍayoḷal-70 by
Mādabōva who had received the village as a gift from the Kadamba ruler who was governing
Banavase-12000 and Pānuṁgal-500.

inscription.head:
No. 99a.
(A.R. No. 42 of 1950-51.)
inscription.p: Same Pillar.

This inscription is dated in the 15th year of the reign of Mallidēva, Khara, Chaitra śu. 5,
Monday. The date corresponds with A.D. 1231, 10th March, Monday.

It records a gift of land to the teacher Śāntivarma for offerings to the god Kaḍambēśvara
at Aḷayasiriyūr by Jñānasiṁgi-gavuḍa when Mallidēva was governing Banavase-12000 and
Hānumgal-500.

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1950-51.)
BAICHAVAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone near the Basavaṇṇa temple.

This inscription belonging to the rule of Kāmadēva is dated his 9th regnal year, Saumya,
Mārgasira, śu • • • • Monday. The date is not verifiable.
It records the death of a hero, Kētaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1950-51.)
AKKIVAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near Kallappa temple.

This inscription refers itself to the reign of Vīra-Mallidēva a Kadamba king (A.D. 1217-
1259) and is dated Nandāna, Vaiśākha śu. 10, Monday. It may be assigned to 13th century A.D.

It records a gift of wet land to the priest of Bhuvanēśvara. Mentions the teacher Vāmaśakti
of Bikēsara.

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1950-51.)
HIRĒKAṆGI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Hanumān temple.

This inscription refers itself to the reign of Mallidēva year 10, Vyaya, Kārttika ba. 14,
Thursday.

This nishidhige stone records the observance of the vow of sanyasana and death by
samādhi of Kāḷigāvuṇḍi, a lay-disciple of Dharmasēna.

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1950-51.)
MALLIGGĀR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone kept in the Siddharāmēśvara temple outside the village.
This short inscription is dated Vikrama, Chaitra ba. 1, Sunday. The date is irregular.

It registers the death in a fight of Bēyi-Nāyaka who bore the epithet kāḍānemalla (wrestler
with wild elephant) among others.

l.ka: beyināyaka ba • ya kāḷagadalli vairi māṟāṁtanakaha • • • • •
jitēna labbatē la • • • nāṁ • sa • • gana • • •

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1951-52.)
DOḌVĀḌ, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up near Basavaṇṇa temple outside the village.

The inscription which refers to Vīra-Jayakēsi, is dated in the 12th regnal year, Virōdhi,
Kārttika śu. 1 Thursday.
It records the death of Lahaḍa who was a ḍiṁgariga servant of king Jayakēsi.

l.ka: svasti śrīkādaṁba śrīvīrajayakēśidēvava¨rṣa haṁne[raḍane]ya virōdhi saṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1950-51.)
MISHRIKŌTI, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the Rāmēśvara temple outside the village.
1 The record contains two dates and names of two kings.

The first inscription refers to the reign of Bhūlōkamalla (year 11), Nala, Śrāvana, ba. 3.
Monday. The date corresponds to A.D. 1136, July 18. But the weekday was Saturday.

The record states that Mailaḷa-mahādēvi of the Chālukya family, queen of Jayakēsin, was
ruling the kingdom from her residence at Kuṁdūru. Records a gift of land made by the Ūroḍeya
Mallarasa and Sōvarasa to Bāragiyara Bācheyaṇṇa who rescued them from a foul attack. Bāragi
Bāchaṇṇa assigned the land for offerings etc., to the god Māchēśvara. Pulivara situated in the
Kuntala country is prasied.

inscription.head:
No. 105a.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1950-51.)
inscription.p:
This inscription referring to the reign of Śivachitta Vīra-Permāḍi is dated in his 12th year,
Bahudhānya, Āśvayuja śu. 15, Monday, lunar-eclipse corresponding to A.D. 1158, October 9, the
week day being Thursday.

It records a gift of land made by Daṇḍanāyaka Mallarasa-mahāprabhu of Pulivara, and
prabhu Mallidēva to god Māchēśvara. Vāchaṇa of Puli is mentioned as the slave of god Māchēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1950-51.)
KITTŪR, SAMPAGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the temple of Hoṇḍada Basavaṇṇa.
Dated Kali year 4289, regnal year 15, Durmati Āshādha śu. 8, Sunday. irregular.
Published in JBBRAS, Vol. IX, pp. 304 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1950-51.)
BĪḌI, KHANAPUR TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone built into the tank-bund near the village.

This damaged inscription is dated in the 13th year of the reign of Kadamba Jayakēśi
Siddhārthi, Bhādrapada ba. 5, Sunday. Irregular.
It states that Kāmaya-daṇṇāyaka and refers to the capture of cattle by Habbeya-Nāyaka.

l.ka: raneya sāhaṇiya magaṁ dēvarā • yana ·ādiyaru ṭhāṇāṁtara

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1950-51.)
GŌLIHAḶḶI, KHANAPUR TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Kalamēśvara temple.

This inscription which refers to the reign of Śivachitta Vīra-Permāḍidēva is dated (i) in
the year 14, Vikrama, Vaiśākha śu 10, Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1160, April 17 when the
weekday was Sunday and not Monday; (ii) dated in the year 17, Svabhānu, Mārgaśira ba. 5,
Monday corresponding to A.D. 1163, November 18; and (iii) dated in the year 5, Nandana, Māgha
śu 10, Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1173, January 25. The regnal year given as 5, is obviously
a mistake.

It states that the Kadamba chief was ruling over Konkaṇa-900 and Palasige-12000, and
Vēḷugrāma-70 divisions from his capital at Gove when his subordinate Chāvaṇadaṇḍanātha got
a built a temple of Sōmēśvara at Kiru-Saṁpagāḍi and made a gift of lands, garden, houses and
oil for worship and offerings after laving the feet of Śrōtriyadēva. The donor is described as
mahāpradhāna, sēnādhipati, haḍapavaḷa, maneveggaḍe and guptabhaṇḍāri.

Five hundred svāmis of Ayyāvoḷe, the seṭṭis of Piriya Gobbūru, Kunilagere, Seḍiṁba,
Polavase, Purigere, Lokkiguṇḍi, Hānumgalla-nāḍu Mārudige, Gaṅgāpura and Erambarage, the
nānadēsis, the seṭṭis and seṭṭiguttas of Kiru-Sampagāḍi in Halasigenāḍu, and aruvattokkalu made
a grant of income from taxes to the same temple after laving the feet of Śaktiśivadēva, on the
occasion of Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa and lunar eclipse.

The last part of the record registers a gift of income from taxes on merchandise carried on
the backs of bulls, buffaloes and donkeys, to god Sōmēśvaradēva by the seṭṭiguṭṭas, mummuridaṇḍas
and nānādēsis of four paṭṭaṇas who had assembled as Mahānāḍu at Kiru-Sampagāḍi. The record
is published in JBBRAS, Vol. X, pp. 296 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1950-51.)
DĒGAON, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Second slab fixed at the well in the Kamalanārāyaṇa temple.
Published in JBBRAS. Vol. IX, pp. 287 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1950-51.)
DĒGAON, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Third slab fixed at the well Kamalanārāyana temple.
Published in JBBRAS, Vol. IX, pp. 310 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1950-51.)
DĒGAON, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the Kamalanārāyana temple.
Published in JBBRAS, Vol. IX, p. 294.

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1950-51.)
DĒGAON, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the Kamalanārāyana temple.

This inscription states that the pilaster was made by (the sculptor i.e.) Nāgayya. In
characters of about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1951-52.)
MAḌAKI HONNAḶḶI, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the compound of the Kalamēśvara temple.

This badly damaged record, referring to the rule of Śivachitta-Permāḍi, is dated in Śaka
101[8] Dhātu. The details of the date cannot be verified.

It records a gift of land, house, oil-mill etc., for the deity Grāmēśvara, made over into
the hands of Rudraśakti-paṇḍita by Ādityayya, a subordinate of the king who was governing
Koṁkaṇa-900 and Halasigi-12000 from his capital Chaṁdrapura. Records another gift of the deity
Ugurēśvara by the Ugura-300 and others.

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. 35 of 1951-52.)
DOḌVĀḌ, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone lying in the fort.

This badly mutilated inscription dated • • • • • trayōdasi, Monday refers to Śivachitta
Vajradēva and to a Kadamba king. It records the death of a hero in a great fight. Vijayama-
sāhaṇi, son of Rāya-sāhaṇi. Sōḍapa was a ḍiṁgariga of Vajradēva.
The characters are of about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab fixed into a wall of the Kalamēśvara temple.

This mutilated inscription refers itself to the reign of Śivachitta-Vīra-Permāḍi It is dated
• • • vyaya, Āshāḍha, śu. (5) Sunday.

It registers the gift of land to the god Grāmēśvara by the king who is stated to have been
ruling Palasige Twelve-Thousand and Koṁkaṇa-Nine-Hundred. Mentions the deities Bāṇakēśvara
and Byāseśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1950-51.)
BOMBAY, BOMBAY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Prince of Wales Museum.

This inscription refers to the reign of Sāḷuva-Nāyaka and is dated Śaka 1485, Dundubhi,
Chaitra śu. 12, Monday, Magha corresponding to A.D. 1562, March 16, f.d.t. 76, f.d.n. 74.

It records the construction of a shrine at Gerasoppe by the chief Sāḷuva-Nāyaka, son of
Bemmarasi-Nāyikiti and Viṭṭappa-Nāyaka, and the consecreation of Śānti-Tīrthaṅkara therein and
records gifts of lands made to the same by the chief. It also refers to the rule of the queen
Chenna Bhairādēvi over the provinces of Nagire, Haive, Tuḷu and Koṅkaṇa on the west coast.
Reviewed in ASR for 1924-25, p. 115.

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1952-53.)
SAVAṆŪR, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into a wall Prānti office.

This inscription is dated in Śaka 1520, Viḷambi, Kārttika śu. 7, corresponding to A.D. 1598,
November 25, Saturday.

It records the excavation of a well near the village Nīlage by Khāneājam Khursitakhān,
the chief of Baṅkāpur fort, for the merit of king.

l.ka: mada samīpadalli sumuhū¨rthadiṁda kaṭṭisida bāvige maṁgaḷa mahā-
ni¨rjarē dēśē pathichāyādiva¨rjitē || 1 || tatra ku¨rvaṁti yē-
lōkā dha¨rmani¨rvāṇa saṁgrahaḥ || 2 || tatsa¨rvaṁ kathayiṣyāmi sa-
vattiṣṭaṁtti mēdinī | tatpuṁṇyāni va¨rddhaṁti tē vasaṁti nabhastalē || dha-
¨rmakā¨ryēṣu vighnāni yē ku¨rvaṁti narādhamā tē vasaṁti yamaḥ

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1951-52.)
POḶALI, MANGALORE TALUK, KANARA SOUTH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in front of the Rājarājēśvari temple.

This inscription engraved in characters of about 7th-8th century A.D., states that Arākoli
entered fire becoming a victim of calumny at Taḻekāḍu. The memorial stone in his honour was
set up by Palyavaṇa Aḍigānapa and Malaloṇḍaiyya.

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. 86 of 1951-52.)
HIRĒKERŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar lying near Toṭada Vīraṇṇa temple.
This damaged record is dated Śaka [881], Siddhārthi, Vaiśākha, śu. 5, Thursday. Irregular.

It states that the stone marks the performance of gōsāsa by a certain person (name not
clear).

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1950-51.)
BOMBAY, BOMBAY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Prince of Wales Museum, Slab No. B. B. 350.

The record of about the 8th-9th century A.D. gives a number of epithets such as Kuḷaniti,
Kaḍugati-sēbya, Sarbba-satyaniratar, etc., of Kōmaḷiga. States that the memorial stone was made by
the stone-cutter Kanne.

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 32 of 1951-52.)
BAILHOṄGAL, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone lying in the Mamaladar's office.

This damaged inscription is dated in [Śaka] 992, Sādhāraṇa, Paushya ba. 5, Friday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti corresponding to A.D. 1070, December 24, Friday.

It records a grant of land to god Māchēśvara by Mahāsāmanta Gaṇḍamarasa. Other gifts
to the same deity are also mentioned. The land so granted was purchased earlier for the said
purpose.

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1950-51.)
DOḶḶEŚVAR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 1) on the tank-bund.

This fragmentary record states that Ēṟegāri who bore the title Nanniyagōva (protector of
truth) among others, was holding the office of Nāḻ-gāvuṇḍa for Bāḍa division. The characters are
of about 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1950-51.)
DOḶḶĒŚVAR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 11) on the tank-bund.

This fragmentary inscription records the performance of a gōsāsa. The characters are of
about 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1952-53.)
IṄGALAḶḶI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing near the Chāvaḍi.

This badly damaged and worn out inscription is engraved in characters of about 10th
century. It seems to refer to the death of a hero (name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1952-53.)
KAMALĀPURA, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Archaeological Range office: Two fragments found near the cistern close to the king's Audience
hall in the ruins at Hampi.

This fragmentary record in characters of about 10th century A.D. contains verses in praise
of Jainamunis Guṇachandramuni, Indranandimuni and probably their disciple a lady who had
obtained Sanyāsa had become a ‘Kaṅti’ [a Jaina lady ascetic].

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1951-52.)
MŪḶŪRU, (hamlet of Bāḷepuṇi), MANGALURU TALUK, KANARA SOUTH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept in the courtyard of the Īśvara temple.

This damaged and worn out inscription is in characters of about 10th century A.D.
Introduces two chiefs of the Kiḷḷa family Kannayya and his brother Vikramāditya, who bore the
title Mūlapuraparamēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1951-52.)
MŪḶŪRU (hamlet of Bāḷepuṇi), MANGALURU TALUK, KANARA SOUTH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Back of the same slab.

This damaged and worn out inscription is in characters of about 10th century A.D. Seems
to refer to the death of Kiḷḷa Vikramāditya and to the construction of a tank.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 219 of 1952-53.)
KĀMALĀPURAM, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Archaeological Range office: Fragment of a slab found near the Zanana enclosure in the ruins
Hampi.

This fragmentary inscription contains some verses in praise of an ascetic (muni). The
characters belong to about 10th-11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 221 of 1952-53.)
KĀMALĀPURAM, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Archaeological Range office: Fragment found near the king's Audience Hall in the ruins at
Hampi.

This fragmentary record in characters of about 11th century A.D. seems to contain some
verses in praise of Narēṁdra Sēna.

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 46 of 1951-52.)
HOMBAḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone kept outside the Saṅkaraliṅga temple.

This damaged and worn out inscription is dated in the cyclic year Śaka 923, Plava, Chaitra,
śu. 10, Sunday corresponding to A.D. 1001, April 6, Sunday. Seems to record the death of hero
(name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 218 of 1952-53.)
KURUVATTI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On wall behind the sculpture of royal personage at the southern entrance of the Mallikārjuna
temple.

This damaged record is dated in the Chālukya-Vikrama year 11, Naḷa, Āshāḍha Puṇṇame,
Monday, lunar eclipse, Vyātipāta (Irregular).

The inscription records a gift of land for a flower-garden by the Ūroḍeyas, etc., of the
Brahmapuri village Kuṟuvatti after laving the feet of Abhinava Vā[ji]dēva, who seems to be an
ascetic of the Kālamukha order.

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 287 of 1952-53.)
ANUGONḌA, KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone near the gāḍi-bhāvi.

This damaged record is dated in the Chālukya year 49, [Pra]mādi, Vishu-saṅkrānti, the
other details being lost. It corresponds to A.D. 1125.

It states that Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kūcha • • rala, brother of Ghaṭeyaṁka • • , granted a
piece of land for the expenses of offerings and worship to god Bhīmēśvara. The gift is stated to
have been made for the merit of Uttama-Chōḷadēva and Kūchādēvi. The donor bears a string of
Telugu-Chōḷa birudas commencing with the introduction charaṇa-sarōruha, etc.

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 43 of 1950-51.)
ARḶĒŚVAR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar on the proper right side of the Kadaṁbēśvara temple.

This inscription is dated Chālukya Vikrama-kāla 61, Nala, Chaitra ba. 5, Tuesday, Mēsha-
saṁkrānti corresponding to A.D. 1136, March 24, Tuesday.

It registers the gift of oil seeds (eḷḷu) made for burning a perpetual lamp before god
Kaḍaṁbēśvara by several seṭṭis (names specified) of the (guild of) Five-Hundred, after laving the
feet of their preceptor Gaṁgarāśi-paṇḍita. Another gift of oil was made on the same occasion by
other seṭṭis and the Seventy Families for the same purpose. Hiṭṭōja engraved the record.

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1950-51.)
DĒGAON, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up near the Basavaṇṇa temple.

This badly damaged record is dated in Śaka 10[81], Pramāthin, Mā[rgaśira] śu. 11, Monday
corresponding A.D. 1159, November 23, Monday.

Seems to record the death of a chief in a cattle-raid. Manneya Nāgaṇa probably set up
the stone.

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1952-53.)
KURUVATTI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the capital of a pillar, proper left in the Mallikārjuna temple.

It contains praises of two brothers, Guḍayyaseṭṭi and KanṇIseṭṭi and seems to record a gift
of one mattar of land out of the 26 mattars to god Āha[va]mallēśvara.
The characters belong to about 11th-12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1952-53.)
AMMINABHĀVI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pedestal of a Jaina image in the Jaina temple.

This record of about 12th century A.D. states that the idol of Chandranāthasvāmi was got
prepared by Bammi-seṭṭi son of Pāri-seṭṭi of the Sēnagaṇa and Mūlasaṁgha for the Jaina temple.

l.ka: śrīmūlasaṁgha sēnagaṇada pāriseṭṭiyara maga tama • da bammiseṭṭi • • basadi-

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. II) set up in the Grāmadēvata temple.

This portion of a broken and damaged record seems to contain the description of a
family of governors. Mentions Kāmadēva, Prabhu-Malla, Māyidēva and Māchidēva. Mahādēva-
daṇḍa-nāyaka, the mahapradhāna, Paṭṭa-Sāhani and commender of forces was governing a province
[name lost].
