inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Parapet wall to the right of entrance into the Sabhāmaṇṭapa of the Sōmēśvara temple.

This inscription is engraved below three groups of sculptures. States that the sculptures
depict the scenes of (i) Jēḍara Dāsimayya offering cloth to the god; (ii) Siriyāḷi-śetti and
Cheṁgaḷavve offering their son to the god: (iii) god Śiva as having come down and danced before
the kuṁbāra (i.e. potter) Guṁḍa. In characters of about 12th century A.D.
Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXIX p. 139 ff.

l.ka:
© jēḍaradāsimayyaṁgaḷu dēvarige vastrava kuḍuva ṭā·u || © siriyāḷaseṭṭi
caṁgaḷavvedēvarige magananikkuva ṭā·u || kuṁbāragu[ṁ]ḍana muṁde

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 59 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Parapet wall to the right of entrance into the Sabhāmaṇṭapa of the Sōmēśvara temple.

This inscription, engraved above panel sculptures, states that the sculptures represent the
exploits of Ēkāntada Rāmayya against the followers of the Jina. The characters are of about 12th
century A.D.
Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. V, p. 260-61.

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Right side of the entrance into the inner shrine of the Sōmēśvara temple.

This inscription, engraved above panel sculptures, states that the sculptures depict the scene
of Ekāntada Rāmayya breaking the Jina and setting up the Śiva-liṅga. The characters are of about
12th century A.D.
Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXIX, p. 139.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Left side of the entrance into the inner shrine of the Sōmēśvara temple.

This inscription, engraved above a panel of sculptures, states that sculptures depict the
scene of Śaṁka-gāvuṁda confering the certificate (of victory) to the illustrious Ēkāntada Rāmayya.
In characters of about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. I) set up in the compound of the Basavēśvara temple.

This damaged stone about 12th century A.D. seems to record the death of a warrior in a
fight details of which are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1950-51.)
AKKIVAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab standing near the tank close to the village.

This damaged record mentions a Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara and registers a gift of land and gar-
den; details lost.
The characters belong to about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 47 of 1950-51.)
BAICHAVAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 11) set up near the Hanumān temple.

This inscription is very much damaged and worn out. The characters are of about 12th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 46 of 1950-51.)
BAICHAVAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 1) found in front of the Hanumān temple.

This fragmentary inscription dated 12th year seems to record the death of a person. The
characters belong to the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 34 of 1951-52.)
BEḶAVĀDI, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the drain of Haḷekōṭa (a garden) outside the village.

This very badly damaged inscription is dated in the month Phālguṇa śu. 5, Sunday. Seems
to register a gift of gold. The characters are of about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1950-51.)
BĪḌI, KHANAPUR TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up near the temple of Bākavva.

This inscription registers the death, in a fight, of Hanmana Sūreya-Nāyaka who had
obtained the favour of god Brahmayadēva and goddess Kāḷikādēvi. Ruvāri Ivōja engraved the
record. The characters belong to about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 74 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone near the Vīrabhadra temple.

This damaged and worn out inscription records the setting up of the hero-stone in memory
of someone whose name is not clear.
The characters of the record belong to the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone near the Hanumān temple.

This fragmentary and worn out inscription is not clear. Seems to state that Īśvara was
holding the office of gāmuṇḍu.
The characters belong to about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 80 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Third slab lying near the Hirēmaṭha.

The upper portion of this record is worn out completely. In the lower portion is partially
preserved a long list of vṛittis granted to several persons: Bhāskara-bhaṭṭa, Vishṇu-bhaṭṭa, Gōhaḷisara-
bhaṭṭa, Gaṇapati-bhaṭṭa, Kēsari-bhaṭṭa, Sōmana-bhaṭṭa, Vīraṇa-bhaṭṭa, Gauthama Gōvinda-bhaṭṭa,
Brahmadēvara-bhaṭṭa, Vishṇuvardhanadēva, Saṅgamēśvara-bhaṭṭa, Dēva-bhaṭṭa, Lingaṇa-bhaṭṭa,
Virupaksha-bhaṭṭa, Lakshmidhara-bhaṭṭa, Siddanātha-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya, Harihara-bhaṭṭa, Sirudēva-
bhaṭṭa, Narasimha-bhaṭṭa, Rāya-bhaṭṭa, Srirāma-bhaṭṭa, Sōmanatha-bhaṭṭa, Kēsava-bhaṭṭa, Sēvaṇa-
bhaṭṭa, Nāgana-bhaṭṭa, Shēkara-bhaṭṭa.
The characters belong to about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1951-52.)
DOḌVĀḌ, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up in the market place.

This is a fragmentary and wornout inscription. It seems to record the death of a warrior
in a fight (details not clear). The characters belong to about 12th century A.D.

l.ka: • • • • • ki • • • • • paṇna • paḍe • kādu • • • • • bi • • • • • ḷama

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 59 of 1950-51.)
HIRĒKAṆGI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in front of the Hanumān temple.

This damaged inscription is dated in the cyclic year Nandana, Mārgasira, śu • • •, Uttarāyaṇa
saṁkrānti.

It registers a gift of land for offerings, repairs etc., of a temple and for feeding the ascetics
and others. Mentions Pānuṁgalanāḍu.
The characters belong to about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1950-51.)
HIRĒKAṆGI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone lying on a tank called Kārikihoṇḍa, outside the village.

This damaged record registers the death of a seṭṭi (name not clear). The characters are of
about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 45 of 1951-52.)
HOMBAḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up in the Śaṅkaraliṅga temple.

This hero-stone of about 12th century A.D. commemorates the death of a hero, Māguḍa-
Mallaya-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 47 of 1951-52.)
HOMBAḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone built into the wall of the Hirēmaṭha.

This is a fragmentary inscription. Only a part of grant portion is preserved. Mentions
Chāvuṇḍamayya-nāya[ka], the āchārya and the [Hundred] and Twenty [mahājanas]. The characters
belong to about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1951-52.)
HOMBAḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Back of the Hanumān image in a shrine at Yallāpur (old site near Hombaḷ).

This badly damaged inscription in characters of about 12th century A.D., contains an
elaborate description of a fight in which Habbaṇa-daṇḍanātha died. Seems to record some gift
made in this connection by Purushōttama-daṇḍanātha on the representation of the prabhu-gāvuṇḍas
of the four villages and the mahājanas of Poṁboḷal, to the Brahmajinalaya. There is a referenceto
Kalachuri Bijjala-dēva.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1950-51.)
HŌTANAHAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 1) set up near the Kallappa temple.

This fragmentary record refers to the death of a person name lost. The characters belong
to about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1950-51.)
HŌTANHAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone No. II, Kallappa temple.

This much damaged and worn out inscription is dated in the cyclic year Sarvadhāri. It
mentions Hannahaḷḷi. The characters belong to about 12th century A.D.

l.ka: śrī maccāḷukya • da • mā • • ka sa¨rvadhāri

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1950-51.)
KIḶKUYILKUḌI, (KIḶAKKUḌI) MADURAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Rock to the north of the base-relief of Jaina images in Pēchchippaḷḷam Samaṇarmalai
hill near the stone lamp post.

This inscription mentions the Jaina teachers Āriyadēva, Baḷachandradēva of Beḷaguḷa, who
belonged to the Mūla-saṁgha, Nēmidēva, Ajitasēnadēva and Gōvardhanadēva. The characters are
of about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1952-53.)
KŌḶIVĀḌ, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up in the compound of Kalamēśvara temple.
Damaged and worout. In characters of 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1952-53.)
KŌḶIVĀḌ, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragmentary stone above the sculpture of a swan, lying near Kalamēśvara temple.

This record states that it is the dharma of the Two Hundred and the Ūrōḍeya, caused to
be made by Sōvarāsi of Kōḷivāḍa. The characters are of about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1952-53.)
MALIGIVĀḌ, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone pedestal bearing footmarks in the Śiva temple.

This damaged and worn out record mentions description of a Jaina teacher. The characters
are of about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1952-53.)
MALIGIVĀḌ, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken stone set up near the Śiva temple.

This badly damaged record mentions Sōmagauḍa of Chikkagēri, an ornament of the
paṭṭagāṟa family. The characters belong to the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1950-51.)
MALLIGGĀR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Part of a stone beam in the Siddhēśvara temple.

This fragmentary inscription of about 12th century A.D. contains a poetic description of
some region.

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1952-53.)
MAṆṬŪR, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the compound of Vallabhēśvara temple.

Damaged and worn out. Appears to be a Jaina record. In characters of about the 12th
century.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1952-53.)
MAṆṬŪR, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the compound of Vallabhēśvara temple.

Damaged and worn out. Appears to be a Jaina record. In characters of about the 12th
century.

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1950-51.)
RĀMATĪRTHA, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing near the Rāmēśvara temple.

This fragmentary record refers to the Hoysaḷa king Vishṇuvardhana and his son Narasimha.
The characters belong to the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1950-51.)
RĀMATĪRTHA, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up in the temple of Rāmasvāmin.

This damaged inscription refers to a feudatory bearing the title Banavāsi-puravarādhīsvara
and to a certain anādiyagrahāra.
The characters belong to about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1952-53.)
SAVAṆŪR, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab standing near the Dyāmavva temple.
This damaged inscription is in characters of about 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. 59 of 1952-53.)
SAVAṆŪR, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Nishidhi stone set up near the Hanumān temple.

This damaged inscription is dated in the cyclic year Viśvāvasu, Suchimāsa ba. 10, Monday.
Seems to record the death of the Jaina teacher Maladhāri by saṁnyasana vidhi. The characters
are of about 12th century.

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Hiremaṭha.

This inscription is worn out. Seems to register a gift of one mattar land and mentions the
Ūroḍeya (name lost). The characters belong to about 13th century A.D.

l.ka: śrī nā • • yaḷemata • sadra • mane • mi

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. 31 of 1951-52.)
IṬAGI, KHANAPUR TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the village.

This damaged inscription, in characters of about 13th century A.D., is almost completely
effaced.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. 237 of 1950-51.)
LADDAGIRI, KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone near dharmachāviḍi.

This damaged record mentions a Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara. The details of the record are lost.
The characters are of about 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. 85 of 1950-51.)
RĀMATĪRTHA, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken hero-stone kept in the Rāmasvāmin temple.

This fragmentary record of about 13th century A.D. contains description of Ramatīrtha and
a fight.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1951-52.)
RAṬṬIHAḶḶI, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up near the Kēśava temple.

This inscription commemorates the death of Mēdara Pāṁḍya near Bisilahaḷḷi when the
teacher Mūṟujāvidēva was proceeding to Huligeṟe. The children of the deceased were compensated
with a gift of land.
In characters of about 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1950-51.)
SURḶĒSHVAR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in a garden outside the village.

Records a gift of land as taḷavṛitti by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tailahadeva to the god
Chikēśvara. The characters belong to the 13th century.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1950-51.)
YELLĀPUR, YELLAPUR TALUK, KANNARA NORTH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in a field outside the village.

This damaged inscription in characters of about 13th century A.D. is dated in the cyclic
year Dundubhi. Seems to record the death of a person and the setting up of the memorial stone.

l.ka: vīra nānibīra • ya • bitā • • • • • bi • dīra • • • • •
• ·agū • • • bīragallige maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. 294 of 1952-53.)
KĀRUMAÑCHI, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in front of the Bhōgēśvara temple.

This damaged record is dated in the cyclic year Pramādi, Vaiśākha, śu. 5. The Śaka year
is not given. Seems a gift of a portion of their dues to the Liṅgada-Vīras by the gauḍas and
prajes of Kārumañchi to Bhōgēśvara.

l.ka: śākha śu 5 lu kārumaṁciya gauḍa mā

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1950-51.)
MISHRIKŌTĪ, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in a field Survey No. 262 outside the village.

This inscription written in characters of about 14th century A.D. states that the land was
a gift made by Elappa-Voḍeya to Maddaḷarasa.

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. 295 of 1952-53.)
KĀRUMAÑCHI, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Lamp post in front of the Bhōgēśvara temple.

This inscription is dated in the cyclic year Pārthiva, Nija-Āshāḍha śu. 5, Thursday
(incomplete). States that Śrīgirinātha set up the lamp post for the merit of his parents, Siṅgana-
gauḍa and Nāgasāni.

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 85 of 1951-52.)
HIRĒKERŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Māsti stone set up near Toṭada Vīraṇṇa temple.

This inscription is dated in Śaka 1360, (in words) Kāḷayukta, Śrāvaṇa ba. 10, Thursday and
corresponds to A.D. 1438, August 14, Thursday f.d.t. 50.

It records the death of the bride of Mākagauḍa, son of Kōṭagāṟa-nayaka by ascending the
funeral pyre (by the rite of sahagamana) of her husband.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1950-51.)
MALLIGGĀR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up near the Siddharāmēśvara temple.

This inscription is dated Śaka 1[3]73, Prajōtpati, Māgha, śu. 3, Sunday. The date corresponds
with A.D. 1452, January 3, Tuesday (if śu) or February 8, Tuesday (if ba).
It states that the inscribed stone was set up by Hoṁnappa, son of Chaṁḍe-Nāyaka.

l.ka: lū caṁḍenāykara maga hoṁnappa pratiṣṭeya sāsana sile ·ācaṁdrā-

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. 86 of 1952-53.)
KŌḶIVĀD, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the village Chāvaḍi.

This damaged inscription is dated Śaka 1379, Īśvara, other details being not given. Seems
to record a gift to the god Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1951-52.)
IḌYA, MANGALURU TALUK, KANNARA SOUTH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone kept in the Nāgavana of Lakshminārāyaṇa Bhaṭ.
This damaged and worn out record is dated 140[4]. It seems to mention Mallaya.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1952-53.)
GORRĒPALLE, (OR GORAVANAHAḶḶI, a hamlet of Mōda) HINDUPUR TALUK,
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the north prākāra inside the gōpura of the Mahālakshmī temple.

This inscription is dated in [Śaka] 1411, other details being lost. This badly damaged record
mentions Rāmarāja-Viṭṭhala. The inscription is engraved on very rough surface and the letters are
indistinct and illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 207 of 1951-52.)
ŚRĪRAṄGAM, TIRUCHIRAPALLI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall Prākara, proper left jamb at the western entrance into the Chandana-maṇḍapa.

This inscription is dated in Śaka 1413, Virōdhikrit, Phālguṇa, śu. 10, Monday. These details
are irregular. If śu. 10 is a mistake for śu. 1, the details regularly correspond to 1492 A.D., February
8.

Registers a gift of four villages Kōramaṅgala, Ariyamaṅgalapaṭṭi and two other village
(name not clear) as sarvamānya to the god for feeding Brāhmaṇas daily at Śrīraṅga. The donor's
name is lost.

l.ka: dalū sērsida bīḍuvēli 4 vaḷadilaṁbiṭṭina cauḍaḍiya yeḍe-

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. 339 of 1950-51.)
SRĪRAṄGAM, TIRUCHIRAPALLI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Raṅganāthasvāmi temple II Prākara, South wall.

This inscription is dated in Śaka [14]15, the cyclic year Dundubhi, Kārttika śu. 15,
Wednesday, lunar eclipse.

Seems to record a gift of land after purchasing it for 25 varāha for supplying garlands,
coconuts, betel leaves etc to the god Śrīraṅgadēva by Chikka Narasāṇa-Nāyaka, son of Nāyaka. In
characters of about 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in Survey No. 119 near the village.
This inscription states that it is the gift land (vritti) of Kavi Kōnēri.

The characters belong about 15th century A.D. Figure of Vāmana is carved on the upper
protion.

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 55A of 1952-53.)
BIJAPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the Archaeological Museum.

The record is dated Śaka 1422, Siddhārthin, Bhādrapada ba. 3. Seems to describe the arrangment
made for the cultivation of fallow land, etc., under specified conditions, when Khaneajamamuta-
bāṅe-mojama Gālibakhāna was holding charge of the Thāne-mamale Mahamudābāda.

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 78 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the Hiremaṭha.

This damaged inscription indifferently engraved is dated the year Tā[ra]ṇa, Āśvija ba; the
Śaka year is not given.

It mentions a gift made to the maṭha of Pañchāksharadēva. The characters belong to the
15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1951-52.)
DOḌVĀḌ, SAMPGAON TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up near the Kalmaṭha outside the village.

This damaged and wornout inscription seems to record the death of the son (name lost)
of Kallaya-sāhaṇi. Mentions Mahāpradhāna Sōva[rasa]. In characters of about 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1951-52.)
IḌYA, MANGALURU TALUK, KANNARA SOUTH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone kept in the Mahāliṅgēśvarasvāmin temple.
Very much damaged and worn out. Purport not clear. In characters of about 15th century.

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1951-52.)
KUḶĀI, MANGALURU TALUK, KANNARA SOUTH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in the wet land facing the house of Kuchamane Dāsu Pūjāri.

This damaged and worn out inscription is in characters of about 15th century A.D. Seems
to record a gift of land (details not clear) made by the sons of Sindarasa. Mentions Sunanda and
Sōmayadēva of Vāsāpura.

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1950-51.)
KYĀSANŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the Vīrabhadra temple.

This inscription dated Śaka 13 • •, Ānanda, Mārgasira ba• 12 records the death of a gauḍa
(name not clear), son of Mallagauḍa. In characters of about 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1950-51.)
MŪḌŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone standing near the Rāmēśvara temple.

This fragmentary record of about 12th century A.D. mentions the death of Bommōja and
a gift of land. In characters of about 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1952-53.)
SUḶḶA, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the house of Muḷki Pāṭil.

This inscription, the purport of which is not clear, is dated Śaka [1430], Subhānu,
Bhādrapada śu 10. Monday corresponding to A.D. 1521, September 4, Monday. However, the cyclic
year was Vishu, not Subhānu. Seems to record the gift of an aṟavaṭige by Timmapa-nāyaka.
Kulakaraṇi Kēsava comosed the record.

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1951-52.)
KALGHAṬGI, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in a field called Basavayynavara-hola.

This badly damaged inscription is dated in Śaka 1451, Pārthiva, Māgha śu. 1, Makara-saṁ
krānti. The date is irregular. It seems to record a gift of wet land at Kallakuṭage to Arasave,
daughter of sthānapati Saṁjapaṁṇa of the Kauśika-Viśvāmitra-gōtra and Ṛik-śākhā by Rāyasada
Lakhayya under the instructions of Ellapa-Voḍeya.

Above this another record has been engraved which refers to a gift [a land grant] to
Sthānapati Mallarasa. The name of the donor and the date of the record are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 87 of 1952-53.)
KŌḶIVĀḌ, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar standing in front of the Nārāyaṇa temple.

This interesting record is dated Śaka 1485, Rudhirōdgārin, Pushya śu. 15, Wednesday, lunar
eclipse. (1563 A.D. December 29) States that Sūrappayya, son of Āneya Dēvappayya, of Agastya-
gōtra, Āśvalāyana-sūtra and Ṛik-śākhā, who was chief (yajamāna) of the agrahāra of Kōḷivāḍa,
presented a woman named Sūriyamāṇiki as kāṇāchiya-pātra (a hereditary dancing girl) to the
temple of Mādhava-Janārdana for services therein. He also made provision for her livelihood by
means of her receiving from different lands fixed amount of corn and money income of six
ghaṭṭi-varāhas per annum.

ya māḍidanu ·ā heṁṇige māḍisida svāsthejīvita varuṣa 1 kke

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 320 of 1950-51.)
ŚRĪRAṄGAM, TIRUCHIRAPALLI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Prakāra Sēṉaimudaliyar Shrine, North wall.

This is dated in the cyclic year Prajāpati, Śravaṇa ba. 12, Thursday which may be equated
to A.D. 1571 August 16, f.d.t. 39. The Śaka year is not given.
States that Padumarasa, son of Bukkarasa-paṇḍita, visited (and worshipped) god Raṅganātha.

l.ka: prajāpatisaṁvatsarada śrāvaṇaba 12 gu bukkarasapaṁḍitara maga padumarasa-

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1951-52.)
KALGHAṬGI, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing in the field called Khānāpuradavara-hola.

This damaged inscription is dated Śaka [150]2, [Vi]krama, Vaiśākha śu. 11 corresponding to
A.D. 1580, April 25. It seems to record a sarvamānya gift of land to a person (name not clear)
of the Gautama-gōtra and Ṛik-śākhā.

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1950-51.)
CHIKKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the village.

This damaged inscription is dated Śaka 1515, Vijaya, Chaitra śu. 1, corresponding to A.D.
1593, March 23, Friday.
It records a gift of land to Bīragāuṁḍa at Hosahaḷḷi. The details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1950-51.)
MŪḌŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Sculptured stone set up in the village.
This inscription of about 16th century A.D. mentions Hallōja and Sarasvati [• • • • .]

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. 208 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (1) in the underground temple (I) near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.

This two line record of about 16th century A.D. refers to a person named Rāyasta-
Rāmachaṁdra-Dīkshita.

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. 209 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (2) in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.

This two line record of about 16th century A.D. refers to a person named Siṁgama-saṁ
gyarāma.

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. 210 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (3) in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.
This one line record of about 16th century A.D. mentions Maṁgarasaru.

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. 211 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (4), in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.
This one line record of about 16th century A.D. refers to Bāvi-Nāgarasaru.

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (5), in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.

This one line record of about 16th century A.D. refers to a person named Chikka-
Nāgarasaru.

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. 213 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (6), in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.
This one line record of about 16th century A.D. refers to a person named Rāmarasaru.

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. 214 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (7), in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.
This four line record of about 16th century A.D. refers to a person named Rāyasta-
Rāmachaṁdra-dīkshitara Nāgappa.

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (8), in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.
This one line record of about 16th century A.D. refers to Sōmaṁma.

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (9), in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.
This one line record of about 16th century A.D. mentions a person named Timmama.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (10), in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.
This one line record of about 16th century A.D. refers to Tiṁmaṁma.

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. 218 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (11), in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.
This one line record of about 16th century A.D. mentions Sōmaṁma.

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. 219 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Labelled image No. (12), in the underground temple (I), near the Tuṅgabhadrā river.

This four line record of about 16th century A.D. states that Rāyasta-Rāmachaṁdra-dīkshita's
son (maga) was Nāgappa.

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Sahasraliṅga boulder between the two underground temples.

This two line record of about 16th century A.D. states that a certain person name Keṁ
pa-Śaṁkara.

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. 223 of 1950-51.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Sahasraliṅga boulder between the two underground temples.
This one line record of about 16th century A.D• refers to a person named Vikramādi • • .

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 43 of 1951-52.)
HURḶIKUPPI, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone kept near the Basavaṇṇa temple.

The purport of this badly damaged record cannot be made out. Only the cyclic year
Āngirasa is extant.
It is in character of about 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1951-52.)
KALGHAṬGI, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing in the field called Appaṇṇi-hola.

This damaged inscription commences with an obeisance to Śrī-Kṛishṇa and mentions a
Brāhmaṇa of Ṛik-śākhā and Āśvalāyana-sūtra, other details being lost. The characters belong to
about 16th century A.D.

l.ka: ṣaṣṭi¨rvaruśasahaśrāṇi viṣṭāya jāyatē krimi || svasti śrīśu-

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. 54 of 1951-52.)
KALGHAṬGI, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab found in the field of Jaina Upādhya.

It states that the field is a sarvamānya-vṛitti of Kamaḷadē[va]bhaṭṭa, son of Padmanābha-
bhaṭṭa, the Upādhya of Belugāve, who belonged to the Bhāradvāja-gōtra, Āśvalāyana-sūtra and
Ṛik-sākhā.
The characters belong to the 16th century A.D.

ṁ dānāt sva¨rgamavāpnōti pālanādacyutaṁ padaṁ | sva-

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1951-52.)
MŪḶŪRU, MANGALURU TALUK, KANNARA SOUTH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two slabs kept in the courtyard of the Īśvara temple.

This damaged and worn out inscription is in characters of about 16th century A.D. Mentions
Maṅgaḷūru-rājya and Śrīdharadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 100 of 1952-53.)
SUḶḶA, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the house of Hiregauḍa.

This is a damaged inscription. It states that a gift [of land] was made by Timmapa-nāyaka
for having dug a well at Suḷa.
The record belongs to about 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 101 of 1952-53.)
SUḶḶA, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar set up near Hiregauḍa's house.

This damaged record mentions a gift [of land] made to Chandra-bhaṭṭa. The charcters
belong to about 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 85 of 1952-53.)
KIRĒSŪR, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Wooden beam above the door of the Kalamēśvara temple outside the village.
This inscription is dated Śaka 1544, Durmati (no other details).

It states that the door was caused to be made by Liṅgaṇagauḍa, son of Saṅkaṇagauḍa of
Kiṟusūr. Bharamōja was the carpenter who made it.

l.ka: lisida bāgalu yida māḍidāta bharamōja yeṁdu barada baraha ||

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1951-52.)
ABLŪR, KOD TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Māsti (Mahāsati) stone (No. 1) set up on the tank bund near the Basavēśvara temple.
This badly damaged inscription seems to record the death probably of a lady (details lost).

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1950-51.)
AKKIVAḶḶI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab standing near Kallappa temple.

Stone bearing the figure of a liṅga marks the boundary of Akkivaḷḷi. The characters
belong to about 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1951-52.)
MAḌAKI HONNAḶḶI, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in a field near the village.

This inscription is very badly damaged. It seems to state that the wet field was a gift made
by Hiriya-gauḍa of Hōmada-Hoṁnehaḷḷi under instructions from Basavaṁtapayya. The characters
belong to 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1952-53.)
SUḶḶA, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the house of Tīrthagauḍa.

This is a damaged inscription. It records a gift of land made for digging a well on the
boundary of Amalagoḷḷa by Siriyapagauḍa and others of Suḷa. The record is written by Saṅkara.
In characters of about 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 91 of 1950-51.)
SURḶĒSHVAR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the court-yard of the Viraktamaṭha.

This inscription, indifferently written, is dated in Śaka 1701, Viḷambi, Jyēshṭha ba. 1
corresponding to A.D. 1779, June 11, Thursday. Seems to record the construction of the Viraktamaṭha
by Doḍa Basappa.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1950-51.)
DOḶḶĒŚVAR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the village.
This damaged inscription of late characters refers to Sōmaliṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 315 of 1950-51.)
ŚRĪRAṄGAM, TIRUCHIRAPALLI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the South end prākāra of the Sēṉaimudaliyār shrine.
Records the obeisance of Vallabhayya (son ?) of Kuṁmāra Ulirāyadēva.
The record is in Kannada engraved In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1952-53.)
BYĀHAṬṬI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the field called Tōpaṇṇanavara hola.

This damaged inscription of late characters mentions Basavaliṅgadēvara-maṭha and seems
to refer to the construction of the tomb (gaddige) of a teacher by Nāḍagaūḍa Liṅgahara Oḍeya.

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1952-53.)
KĀMALĀPURAM, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Archaeological Range office: A second fragment of a slab found near the Zenana
enclosure in the ruins at Hampi.

This fragmentary inscription is indifferently engraved. In late characters. Seems to mention
Chika-voḍeya and a gift made by him. In late characters.
